vitessio/vitess#18102.body:
### Overview of the Issue

The code to delete a keyspace ignores errors while deleting the shard replication files and the serving keyspace file. If these files are present, then when a new vtgate comes up, it finds that the list of serving keyspaces and tries to rebuild it. Since the keyspace doesn't exist, locking it fails, and this crashes the vtgate altogether. Retrying doesn't help. The users have to currently manually clear the topo fields before the vtgate starts.

Bug introduced by https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/pull/17915

### Reproduction Steps

1. Delete a keyspace and repeat until a residual file is seen in one of the cells.
2. Try bringing up a new vtgate in that cell.

### Binary Version

```sh
main and release-22
```

```sh
-
```

```sh

```

vitessio/vitess#18107.body:
### Feature Description

Discussed with @shlomi-noach and @rohit-nayak-ps.

Currently, once an `OnlineDDL complete` operation is submitted, it isn’t possible to stop or pause it without fully `OnlineDDL cancel`-ing the migration (which stops the copy and removes the data). Considering that `complete` might not immediately succeed on a busy table and will keep retrying indefinitely, it would be helpful to be able to “un-complete” it to have more control over the cutover timing.

### Use Case(s)

Stopping an `OnlineDDL complete` operation that can’t succeed (for example, can’t get a lock on the table) and retrying at a later time without restarting the migration from scratch.
vitessio/vitess#18107.title: Feature Request: Add support for stopping `OnlineDDL complete`

vitessio/vitess#18120.body:
When `fastforward` runs the `stopPos` for `uvStreamer` is set to the gtid of the snapshot taken by the next copy. However it never gets reset. Both `catchup` and `fastforward` use the same callback wrapper where, after processing a transaction, it checks against the `stopPos`

This causes `catchup` to prematurely exit with an `EOF` when there are multiple tables and there are updates or deletes for transactions to catch up on for tables/rows that are already copied during the copy phase.
vitessio/vitess#18120.title: VStream: Reset stopPos in catchup

vitessio/vitess#18125.body: We should add extra log lines in VStream API to try to localize where we are getting the frequent EOF errors we are seeing in a cluster.
vitessio/vitess#18125.title: VStream API: Add additional log lines for debugging frequent eof errors during catchup

vitessio/vitess#18141.body:

vitessio/vitess#18150.body:
### Overview of the Issue

In v22+ VTOrc will start cleanly and report `healthy: true` in it's `/debug/health` health API when it does not have the correct access/ACLs to perform shard locks

Recently we deployed VTOrc to an environment w/Consul topo and a mistakenly-empty Consul static credentials file (broken templating). VTOrc starts fine and reports as healthy, but when it tries to fix a real problem, it encounters this error:
```
Recovery for <Analysis Here> on redacted/-: Failed to lock shard, aborting recovery: deadline exceeded: vitess/global/keyspaces/redacted/shards/-/Lock
```
Of course, when the correct consul credentials are provided, the shard lock is successful

Actually there are 2 x problems to address:
1. VTOrc is considered healthy when in a state that it cannot perform actions
    - Maybe VTOrc should fail to start whatsoever? 🤔 cc @GuptaManan100 
3. `deadline exceeded` is a confusing error considering the issue is access. This might be a Consul-specific gotcha
### Reproduction Steps

1. Start VTOrc with an empty consul credentials json file, ie: `{ }`
2. Call `/debug/health`, notice it is `healthy: true`
3. Generate a real problem for VTOrc to solve, notice it just errors to get the shard lock and and reports `deadline exceeded`

### Binary Version

```sh
Technically v19, but with most v22 VTOrc changes. V22 has the same problem
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
Linux
```

### Log Fragments

```sh
In summary
```
vitessio/vitess#18150.title: Bug Report: VTOrc will report `healthy: true` with bad topo credentials

vitessio/vitess#18154.body:
### Overview of the Issue

It has been noticed on `release-22` and `main`, that if we change the targetted tablet type in a connection to vtgate, then even though there would be a routing rule for that keyspace, tablet type combo, vtgate will fail to respect it.

### Reproduction Steps

1. Start a cluster called ks with a table t1
2. Add a routing rule like 
```
{"rules": [
	{
	"from_table": "ks.t1@replica",
	"to_tables": ["uks.unknown"]
	}
]}
```
3. Start a connection to vtgate.
4. Run `use ks@primary`
5. Run `select * from t1`
6. Run `use ks@replica`
7. Run `select * from t1`. This query should return an error since it sould be routed to a table that doesn't exist.


### Binary Version

```sh
main, release-22.0
```

vitessio/vitess#18160.body:
### Feature Description

Provide the ability for VStream clients to perform a vstream copy for a specific set of tables. For example, a VStream client is streaming for tables t1 & t2, the user only wants to copy t1, and wants t2 to continue streaming events without data loss. The user needs some way to specify:
1. Copy t1
2. Use this gtid1 to continue binlog events for t2

The outcome is that t1 is fully copied and switches over to real-time. And t2 never loses any data. Ideally, the events for t2 will continue to stream during the catch up phase (as if t2 already had it's copy fully completed, ie it streams all rows). It is acceptable if we blocked all events for t2 until the copy was fully done for t1, but that is not ideal.

### Use Case(s)

* Avoid copying very large tables when only one small table of the vstream needs to be copied (right now only is able to copy every table)
* Prevent unnecessary splitting of vstream clients to account for the fact that if they are grouped with a large table they will experience significant downtime while the copy is occurring (more efficient resource usage by grouping more tables together in one vstream)
* Provide better granularity to only vstream copy the necessary tables rather than all of them
vitessio/vitess#18160.title: Feature Request: VStream copy for specific tables

vitessio/vitess#18202.body:
### Overview of the Issue

We've run into a case where a `PlannedReparentShard` operation failed due to a timeout while trying to close the `TableGC` pool during the `DemotePrimary` step of `PlannedReparentShard`, and now the tablet is in an undefined state and can't be demoted, as it always fails with the timeout error during `DemotePrimary`.

Debugging this, we believe this is because `TableGC` re-uses the same `Pool` instance. This is problematic, because if `Pool.Close` fails once, the pool will be in an undefined state, and re-opening and closing the pool will fail continuously because the `active` connection count on the pool instance will be out-of sync with the "actual" pool state.

I feel like re-using a connection pool instance after calling `.Close`, especially if that `.Close` call ran into a timeout, is not safe.

I believe after calling `.Close`, we should remove any references to the pool, and create a new pool instance when the pool needs to be reopened so that we start with a fresh connection pool instance.

### Reproduction Steps

N/A

### Binary Version

```sh
v19+
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
N/A
```

### Log Fragments

```sh
N/A
```
vitessio/vitess#18202.title: Bug Report: Connection Pool Reuse leads to consistent `PRS` failure

vitessio/vitess#18207.body:
### Overview of the Issue

We have been benchmarking ungraceful leader elections via vtorc and observed that even in an idle setup(cluster with no load) the leader failure detection and mitigation time varies over a large window. We have observed that in many cases, vtorc detects leader unavailability within 10 seconds(via vtorc logs) but still does not perform the mitigation for as long as another 60 seconds.

This seems to be happening because of a lock contention when multiple vtorc instances perform different analyses and block the correct action from being performed. We have seen this happening majorly for DeadPrimary and ReplicationStopped (and some other failures).

There is an existing logic to detect if the identified analysis is because of a different reason but this happens within a lock and adds a significant delay. Additionally, this gets worse if there are multiple vtorc instances because all of these instances will perform the same step and blocks the correct action for a longer duration.

We propose that this check should be moved outside of the lock so that the correct mitigation can be performed quickly. We tried to validate this theory and ran a load test with a patch. We found that with a load of 5K updates/second, vtorc can mitigate a dead leader in under 15 seconds consistently.

### Reproduction Steps

Consider a deployment with 2 vtorc instances(let's call them A and B). 

1. Time to -> A checks the leader's health and finds that the leader is healthy
2. Time t1 -> Leader goes down
3. Time t2 -> A checks replica and finds replication is stopped(because leader is down)
    - A starts mitigation for ReplicationStopped and takes a lock on the shard. 
4. Time t3 -> B finds out that the leader is down for the shard and attempts mitigation but cannot acquire the lock.
5. Timr t4 -> A checks if the leader is down as part of ReplicationStopped mitigation and finds that the leader is down. It then releases the lock.
6. Time t5 -> B acquires the lock starts DeadPrimary mitigation and performs a leader election.

In practice, we have seen that step 5 takes a significant amount of time (in order of 10s-15s) and this seems to delay the leader mitigation.

This can happen with a single vtroc instance also.

### Binary Version

```sh
Version: 2e5327f04008fc166921c126468f389d2dc5a29f 
Git Branch: release-21.0
```

vitessio/vitess#18209.body:
### Overview of the Issue

When the throttler on vttablet encounters a topo watch error (e.g. zookeeper closing the connection) it doesn’t recreate the watch on the SrvKeyspace, so it never gets any more throttler config updates.

In the vttablet logs we see this error message:
```
E0425 10:45:01.036900   40711 throttler.go:381] WatchSrvKeyspaceCallback error: ResilientWatch stream failed for zone1.commerce: zk: zookeeper is closing
```

That comes from here:
https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/68242a621b5c2a92ee19b92fb2cbcffd797e6ef9/go/vt/vttablet/tabletserver/throttle/throttler.go#L379-L384

The problem is that callback function returns `false` when called with an error, and it looks like when a callback function returns `false` it effectively gets removed from the list of listeners here: 
https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/68242a621b5c2a92ee19b92fb2cbcffd797e6ef9/go/vt/srvtopo/watch.go#L164-L171


### Reproduction Steps

I can reproduce this easily using zookeeper as the topo server because connection errors get surfaced as watch errors. I'm not sure how to force a watch error with etcd.

* Start a cluster using zookeeper as the topo server
  * `TOPO=zk2 ./101_initial_cluster.sh`
* Tail the vttablet logs
* Enable the throttler: 
  * `vtctldclient --server localhost:15999 UpdateThrottlerConfig --enable --threshold 1.0 commerce`
  * After a few seconds you should see something like this in the vttablet logs:
    ```
    I0425 10:43:45.794495   40711 throttler.go:425] Throttler: applying topo config: enabled:true threshold:1
    I0425 10:43:45.794520   40711 throttler.go:531] Throttler: enabling
    ```
* Stop zookeeper
  * `TOPO=zk2 CELL=zone1 ../common/scripts/zk-down.sh`
  * You should see something like this in the vttablet logs
    ```
    E0425 10:45:01.033478   40711 watch.go:211] ResilientWatch stream failed for zone1.commerce: zk: zookeeper is closing
    received a non-OK event for /vitess/zone1/keyspaces/commerce/SrvKeyspace
    E0425 10:45:01.036900   40711 throttler.go:381] WatchSrvKeyspaceCallback error: ResilientWatch stream failed for zone1.commerce: zk: zookeeper is closing
    ```
* Start zookeeper
  *  `TOPO=zk2 CELL=zone1 ../common/scripts/zk-up.sh`
* Modify the throttler config:
  * `vtctldclient --server localhost:15999 UpdateThrottlerConfig --enable --threshold 2.0 commerce`
  * The change will never be seen by the vttablet



### Binary Version

```sh
Impacts main and earlier versions
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
N/A
```

### Log Fragments

```sh

```
vitessio/vitess#18209.title: Bug Report: Throttler config updates are not seen by vttablet after topo watch error

vitessio/vitess#18227.body:
**Issue Description**
In a large MoveTables workflow (128 → 128 shards, over 140 billion rows), we encountered persistent "Deadline exceeded" errors when running a VDiff operation. The VDiff job failed to complete even after running for over 24 hours.
```
Error: (shard 2e-30) deadline exceeded: /vitess/vitess-loadtest-db/global/keyspaces/customer_v8/locks
Error: (shard 38-3a) deadline exceeded: /vitess/vitess-loadtest-db/global/keyspaces/customer_v8/locks
```

**Workaround**
We were able to successfully complete the VDiff by manually:

- Stopping all VDiff streams
- Restarting each stream one at a time
- Waiting for each individual stream to complete before starting the next

Using this approach, the full VDiff completed within approximately 5 hours.
```
VDiff Summary for customer_v8.MigrateData (40e02a01-1fdb-11f0-b0e1-36db5969c9c9)
State:        completed
RowsCompared: 141427827192
HasMismatch:  false
StartedAt:    2025-04-23 18:39:56
CompletedAt:  2025-04-23 22:56:20
```

**Suggestion**
It would be beneficial for Vitess to support batched or sequential scheduling of VDiff streams automatically, to avoid lock contention, which seem to be a bottleneck in large-scale workflows.

**Environment**
Vitess version: v16
vitessio/vitess#18227.title: Improve scheduling VDiff streams to speed up VDiff time.

vitessio/vitess#18238.body:
When issuing a query such as:

```sql
select *
from tbl
where a and b member of (c)
```

we can see that the query being sent down to MySQL as:

```sql
select *
from tbl
where (a and b) member of (c)
```

This is clearly wrong, and leads to incorrect results being returned.
vitessio/vitess#18238.title: Precedence for MEMBER OF is incorrect

vitessio/vitess#18247.body:
### Overview of the Issue

I'm trying to do region-based sharding on Kubernetes using the Vitess operator. I'm creating a very simple cluster using only 2 vttablet (primary/replica - no shards) in a keyspace called `main`. In this keyspace, I create the `customer` table provided in the examples from the Git repository and populate it. Then I apply my sharding VSchema. However, when I try to create my lookup Vindex, I get an error on my stream: **`vttablet: rpc error: code = Unknown desc = table customer not found`**.
I don't understand — the `customer` table definitely exists in the `main` keyspace 🤔 🤷‍♂ 

### Reproduction Steps

I am using the files contained in the `examples/operator` and `examples/region_sharding` directories from version 21 of Vitess : https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/tree/release-21.0/examples

1. I am deploying the Vitess operator :


```
❯ kubectl apply -f 101_initial_cluster.yaml
vitesscluster.planetscale.com/example created
secret/example-cluster-config created
```

_101_initial_cluster.yaml_:
```

kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: example-cluster-config
type: Opaque
stringData:
  countries.json: |
    {
      "United States": 1,
      "Canada": 2,
      "France": 64,
      "Germany": 65,
      "China": 128,
      "Japan": 129,
      "India": 192,
      "Indonesia": 193
    }
  users.json: |
    {
      "user": [{
        "UserData": "user",
        "Password": ""
      }]
    }
  init_db.sql: |
    # This file is executed immediately after mysql_install_db,
    # to initialize a fresh data directory.

    ###############################################################################
    # Equivalent of mysql_secure_installation
    ###############################################################################
    # We need to ensure that super_read_only is disabled so that we can execute
    # these commands. Note that disabling it does NOT disable read_only.
    # We save the current value so that we only re-enable it at the end if it was
    # enabled before.
    SET @original_super_read_only=IF(@@global.super_read_only=1, 'ON', 'OFF');
    SET GLOBAL super_read_only='OFF';

    # Changes during the init db should not make it to the binlog.
    # They could potentially create errant transactions on replicas.
    SET sql_log_bin = 0;
    # Remove anonymous users & disable remote root access (only allow UNIX socket).
    DROP USER IF EXISTS ''@'%', ''@'localhost', 'root'@'%';

    # Remove test database.
    DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS test;

    ###############################################################################
    # Vitess defaults
    ###############################################################################

    # Vitess-internal database.
    CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS _vt;
    # Note that definitions of local_metadata and shard_metadata should be the same
    # as in production which is defined in go/vt/mysqlctl/metadata_tables.go.
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _vt.local_metadata (
      name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
      value VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
      db_name VARBINARY(255) NOT NULL,
      PRIMARY KEY (db_name, name)
      ) ENGINE=InnoDB;
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _vt.shard_metadata (
      name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
      value MEDIUMBLOB NOT NULL,
      db_name VARBINARY(255) NOT NULL,
      PRIMARY KEY (db_name, name)
      ) ENGINE=InnoDB;

    # Admin user with all privileges.
    CREATE USER 'vt_dba'@'localhost';
    GRANT ALL ON *.* TO 'vt_dba'@'localhost';
    GRANT GRANT OPTION ON *.* TO 'vt_dba'@'localhost';
    GRANT PROXY ON ''@'' TO 'vt_dba'@'localhost' WITH GRANT OPTION;

    # User for app traffic, with global read-write access.
    CREATE USER 'vt_app'@'localhost';
    GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, DROP, RELOAD, PROCESS, FILE,
      REFERENCES, INDEX, ALTER, SHOW DATABASES, CREATE TEMPORARY TABLES,
      LOCK TABLES, EXECUTE, REPLICATION CLIENT, CREATE VIEW,
      SHOW VIEW, CREATE ROUTINE, ALTER ROUTINE, CREATE USER, EVENT, TRIGGER
      ON *.* TO 'vt_app'@'localhost';

    # User for app debug traffic, with global read access.
    CREATE USER 'vt_appdebug'@'localhost';
    GRANT SELECT, SHOW DATABASES, PROCESS ON *.* TO 'vt_appdebug'@'localhost';

    # User for administrative operations that need to be executed as non-SUPER.
    # Same permissions as vt_app here.
    CREATE USER 'vt_allprivs'@'localhost';
    GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, DROP, RELOAD, PROCESS, FILE,
      REFERENCES, INDEX, ALTER, SHOW DATABASES, CREATE TEMPORARY TABLES,
      LOCK TABLES, EXECUTE, REPLICATION SLAVE, REPLICATION CLIENT, CREATE VIEW,
      SHOW VIEW, CREATE ROUTINE, ALTER ROUTINE, CREATE USER, EVENT, TRIGGER
      ON *.* TO 'vt_allprivs'@'localhost';

    # User for slave replication connections.
    # TODO: Should we set a password on this since it allows remote connections?
    CREATE USER 'vt_repl'@'%';
    GRANT REPLICATION SLAVE ON *.* TO 'vt_repl'@'%';

    # User for Vitess filtered replication (binlog player).
    # Same permissions as vt_app.
    CREATE USER 'vt_filtered'@'localhost';
    GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, DROP, RELOAD, PROCESS, FILE,
      REFERENCES, INDEX, ALTER, SHOW DATABASES, CREATE TEMPORARY TABLES,
      LOCK TABLES, EXECUTE, REPLICATION SLAVE, REPLICATION CLIENT, CREATE VIEW,
      SHOW VIEW, CREATE ROUTINE, ALTER ROUTINE, CREATE USER, EVENT, TRIGGER
      ON *.* TO 'vt_filtered'@'localhost';

    # custom sql is used to add custom scripts like creating users/passwords. We use it in our tests
    # {{custom_sql}}

    # We need to set super_read_only back to what it was before
    SET GLOBAL super_read_only=IFNULL(@original_super_read_only, 'ON');
  rbac.yaml: |
    rules:
    - resource: "*"
      actions:
        - "get"
        - "create"
        - "put"
        - "ping"
      subjects: ["*"]
      clusters: ["*"]
    - resource: "Shard"
      actions:
        - "emergency_failover_shard"
        - "planned_failover_shard"
      subjects: ["*"]
      clusters: ["*"]
```
 
3. I am deploying the following schema :

`vtctldclient ApplySchema --sql="$(cat create_main_schema.sql)" main`

_create_main_schema.sql_:

vitessio/vitess#18251.body:
### Feature Description

VTOrc [has some REST-style HTTP APIs endpoints](https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/main/go/vt/vtorc/server/api.go#L43-L52) that return data in JSON format

While this API approach is functional, there is no client for this API, meaning users that rely on it need to create an API client following no real spec - you just need to read the source and match what comes out. I'm also not sure how documented these APIs really are

This issue requests that the APIs VTOrc supports are formalized as gRPC RPCs/messages. Once they exist, in a follow-up issue I may propose some of these RPCs are callable via `vtctldclient`, but for now having REST/gRPC parity would be fantastic

Following a full integration of gRPC APIs/RPCs I would suggest the current REST-style approach is retired, but it's not a must

I look at this as:
1. Define RPC messages in `proto/vtorcdata.proto`
2. Write RPCs that take the RPC messages as input/output
3. (Optional) implement `vtctldclient` RPCs that make sense
4. (Optional) create REST-type API deprecation notice
    - Retire REST-style APIs _(1-2+ releases after deprecation)_

cc @GuptaManan100 / @deepthi for 💡s

### Use Case(s)

VTOrc user that relies on the existing HTTP REST API
vitessio/vitess#18251.title: RFC: gRPC server for VTOrc APIs

vitessio/vitess#18255.body:
### Feature Description

We are hoping to add a little more visibility to the binlog watcher. Particularly, we want to add log statements when the binlog watcher begins streaming and when it closes. 

We also hope to add logging to where the VStreamer engine locks and unlocks. Please see the PR for implementation.

### Use Case(s)

We added these log statements to our downstream Vitess fork so that we can more accurately triage any vttablet outages that may occur.
vitessio/vitess#18255.title: Feature Request: Add logging around binlog watcher actions

vitessio/vitess#18259.body:
### Overview of the Issue

It seems like the suboptimal query planning once fixed by https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/pull/10966 has been broken by https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/pull/11379 and has been broken since.

For example, a query like this:

```sql
SELECT music.id FROM music WHERE music.id IN (SELECT MAX(music.id) FROM music WHERE music.user_id IN (5, 6) GROUP BY music.user_id)
```

Should be mergeable, and should result in at most one query per shard determined via the `music.user_id IN (5, 6` condition, but instead this currently results in multiple queries being send per shard.

We do have various test cases that should cover this in the `select_cases.json` test file, but they all currently verify that the query is _not_ getting merged. 😞 

Weirdly, wrapping the subquery into a derived table causes the subquery to be merged into the outer query correctly:

```sql
SELECT music.id FROM music WHERE music.id IN (SELECT * FROM (SELECT MAX(music.id) FROM music WHERE music.user_id IN (5, 6) GROUP BY music.user_id) _inner)
```

### Reproduction Steps

N/A

### Binary Version

```sh
Since v15 or v16, up to main.
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
N/A
```

### Log Fragments

```sh
N/A
```

vitessio/vitess#18270.body:
### Feature Description

VStreams are long-running GRPC connections. This means that they eventually get consolidated on older vtgate pods and have uneven load distribution. Even with good load balancing this still happens over time. A workaround is the GRPC max connection/age grace setting (available in tablets) but it blindly terminates all connections to rebalance, rather than just those that need it.

The goal here is to support something already in [GRPC](https://grpc.io/docs/guides/custom-backend-metrics/), sending metrics to a client so the client can use for intelligent load balancing. 
> Open Request Cost Aggregation ([ORCA](https://github.com/cncf/xds/blob/main/xds/data/orca/v3/orca_load_report.proto)) is an open standard for conveying backend metrics information. gRPC uses ORCA service and metrics standards and supports two metrics reporting mechanisms:
> * Per-query metrics reporting: the backend server attaches the injected custom metrics in the trailing metadata when the corresponding RPC finishes. This is typically useful for short RPCs like unary calls.
> * Out-of-band metrics reporting: the backend server periodically pushes metrics data, e.g. cpu and memory utilization, to the client. This is useful for all situations: unary calls, long RPCs in streaming calls, or no RPCs. However, out-of-band metrics reporting does not send query cost metrics. The metrics emission frequency is user-configurable, and this configuration resides in the custom load balancing policy.

In our experience CPU utilization is the most important one. Memory is another common choice to send.

### Use Case(s)

Clients can implement their own custom load balancers that can read these metrics and switch hosts as needed, only terminating vstreams when a rebalance is needed (the vtgate host is degraded and maxed out on resources).

It can also be used at stream initialization as well to find the least utilized server.

Once this is available from vtgates, we can update the Debezium client in the [Debezium Vitess Connector](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-connector-vitess) project to use these metrics for better load balancing.
vitessio/vitess#18270.title: Feature Request: Support GRPC Backend Metrics for Efficient Load Balancing with VStreams

vitessio/vitess#18271.title: Bug Report: unsupported opcode: None

vitessio/vitess#18273.body:
### Feature Description

The current vstream architecture is as follows:
* VTTablet
  1. Reads the binlog
  2. Serializes to protobuf
  3. Sens the protobuf event to the VTGate
* VTGate
  1. Deserializes the protobuf event
  2. [Clones](https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/main/go/vt/vtgate/vstream_manager.go#L699) the entire event
  3. Transforms it (e.g., for a [row](https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/main/go/vt/vtgate/vstream_manager.go#L700) changes the keyspace/table name)
  4. Serializes the protobuf event
  5. Sends to the VStream client

This feature request is to optimize these operations, in particular the copy or repeated ser/deser. The goal is to increase the overall throughput of VStream.

Proposals:
* Remove the copy for ROWs - few ways to do this:
  1. Provide a flag that allows clients to receive untransformed rows, this means they will need to do their own table name adjustments. Since the flag is disabled by default, this will only be opted in by clients who will do this extra processing. Most clients (eg Debezium) need to fully deserialize the data anyways. Also, the vast majority of traffic is ROWs, especially during a VStream copy
  2. Adjust the tablet name in the tablet - change it once in the tablet so we don't need to copy/change the event in the vtgate. Possibly need some internal flag for normal vstream replication (does not need table name change) vs external vstream client replication (needs table name change)
  3. Modify the original event - this means there is a side effect/lack of isolation, but it should be much faster than copying the entire content into a new event. It appears no other parts of vstream manager use the event, so this should be safe
* Remove ser/deser - this would be a larger change, and require more of a refactor. If we receive a byte[] and simply pass that on, we never ser/deser
* Allow for direct tablet to vstream client connection (coordinated by the vtgate) - this probably doesn't make sense to do, and completely goes against vitess architecture and abstraction. Listing here simply for completeness

### Use Case(s)

For very large tables with wide rows (eg large text columns) the VStream can have low throughput. This can cause vstream copy to take multiple days to run. Main use case is that VStream copies can be done faster for large tables (eg even split across shads, some shards can be disproportionately larger due to data skew).

The benefits of higher throughput would be for any vstream or vstream copy, no matter the size.
vitessio/vitess#18273.title: Feature Request: Increase max throughput of VStream

vitessio/vitess#18275.body:
### Overview of the Issue

It has been noticed that sometimes the semi-sync monitor can deadlock. 

The deadlock occurs with the following goroutines - 
```go
goroutine 66 [chan send]:
vitess.io/vitess/go/timer.(*Timer).Stop(0x1400060a270?)
	/Users/manangupta/vitess/go/timer/timer.go:151 +0xec
vitess.io/vitess/go/vt/vttablet/tabletmanager/semisyncmonitor.(*Monitor).Close(0x14000203b90)
	/Users/manangupta/vitess/go/vt/vttablet/tabletmanager/semisyncmonitor/monitor.go:157 +0x10c
vitess.io/vitess/go/vt/vttablet/tabletmanager

goroutine 115 [sync.Mutex.Lock]:
internal/sync.runtime_SemacquireMutex(0x140007022a0?, 0xa0?, 0x14000596ea8?)
	/Users/manangupta/.gvm/pkgsets/go1.24.0/global/pkg/mod/golang.org/toolchain@v0.0.1-go1.24.2.darwin-arm64/src/runtime/sema.go:95 +0x28
internal/sync.(*Mutex).lockSlow(0x14000203ba8)
	/Users/manangupta/.gvm/pkgsets/go1.24.0/global/pkg/mod/golang.org/toolchain@v0.0.1-go1.24.2.darwin-arm64/src/internal/sync/mutex.go:149 +0x170
internal/sync.(*Mutex).Lock(...)
	/Users/manangupta/.gvm/pkgsets/go1.24.0/global/pkg/mod/golang.org/toolchain@v0.0.1-go1.24.2.darwin-arm64/src/internal/sync/mutex.go:70
sync.(*Mutex).Lock(...)
	/Users/manangupta/.gvm/pkgsets/go1.24.0/global/pkg/mod/golang.org/toolchain@v0.0.1-go1.24.2.darwin-arm64/src/sync/mutex.go:46
vitess.io/vitess/go/vt/vttablet/tabletmanager/semisyncmonitor.(*Monitor).setIsBlocked(0x14000203b90, 0x1)
	/Users/manangupta/vitess/go/vt/vttablet/tabletmanager/semisyncmonitor/monitor.go:362 +0x84
vitess.io/vitess/go/vt/vttablet/tabletmanager/semisyncmonitor.(*Monitor).checkAndFixSemiSyncBlocked(0x14000203b90)
	/Users/manangupta/vitess/go/vt/vttablet/tabletmanager/semisyncmonitor/monitor.go:177 +0x10c
vitess.io/vitess/go/timer.(*Timer).run(0x1400041d180, 0x1400060a230)
	/Users/manangupta/vitess/go/timer/timer.go:111 +0xec
created by vitess.io/vitess/go/timer.(*Timer).Start in goroutine 66
```

In the first go-routine we are waiting on sending a message on the Timer channel to stop the timer. However, this message isn't going to be processed until the second go-routine finishes because it is running the keephouse function (In this case it is `checkAndFixSemiSyncBlocked`). The second go routine in turn is blocked trying to acquire a mutex the first one is holding.

This deadlock is happening because we are acquiring a lock in the keephouse function that we also hold while calling Close on the timer.

### Reproduction Steps

The deadlock can be reproduced by repeatedly closing and opening the semi-sync-monitor after getting writes to be blocked.

### Binary Version

```sh
main, release-22
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
-
```

### Log Fragments


vitessio/vitess#18335.body:
### Feature Description

**Problem**
Vitess currently supports launching online schema changes (via OnlineDDL) on a per-shard basis, which is useful for limiting the scope of changes and testing safely. However, completing an online schema change (e.g., via CompleteMigration) currently operates only at the keyspace level, making it impossible to finalize a migration for a single shard independently.

This creates challenges in scenarios where we want to test the performance and resource impact of a schema change (such as adding an index) on a specific shard before rolling it out to the rest of the keyspace.

### Use Case(s)

Suppose we have a keyspace with X shards, and want to:

1. Add an index to a table
2. Measure query performance improvements and resource impact on just one shard
3. Limit the blast radius by only completing the migration on that shard

Currently, we can launch the migration on one shard — but we cannot complete it in isolation without affecting others.

### Proposed Enhancement
Enable CompleteMigration to accept shard-specific arguments, so that a user can explicitly complete a migration on a single shard.

### Benefits
1. Enables safer rollouts of schema changes
2. Allows data teams to test and observe the impact of DDLs on a per-shard basis
3. Improves control and observability of online schema workflows
vitessio/vitess#18335.title: Feature Request:  Support shard-specific CompleteMigration for online DDL

vitessio/vitess#18350.body: ### Overview of the Issue During a VTGate shutdown, we frequently observe the following event: > “Service crashed while stopping” On inspecting the container logs, we consistently find the following panic: ``` panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference [signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x10 pc=0x189420c] goroutine 1063399 [running]: vitess.io/vitess/go/vt/topo/etcd2topo.(*Server).Get(0xc00092a1c0, {0x232a0f8, 0xc006500bd0}, {0xc001e2f2f0?, 0x29?}) vitess.io/vitess/go/vt/topo/etcd2topo/file.go:80 +0x8c vitess.io/vitess/go/vt/topo.(*StatsConn).Get(0xc000920780, {0x232a0f8, 0xc006500bd0}, {0xc001e2f2f0, 0x2e}) vitess.io/vitess/go/vt/topo/stats_conn.go:110 +0x18b vitess.io/vitess/go/vt/topo.(*Server).GetShard(0xc00092c240, {0x232a130, 0xc005da3540}, {0xc00005dae8, 0x11}, {0xc0016c72b8, 0x5}) vitess.io/vitess/go/vt/topo/shard.go:184 +0x2b0 vitess.io/vitess/go/vt/discovery.(*keyspaceState).getMoveTablesStatus.func1() vitess.io/vitess/go/vt/discovery/keyspace_events.go:485 +0x6d golang.org/x/sync/errgroup.(*Group).Go.func1() golang.org/x/sync@v0.10.0/errgroup/errgroup.go:78 +0x50 created by golang.org/x/sync/errgroup.(*Group).Go in goroutine 4361 golang.org/x/sync@v0.10.0/errgroup/errgroup.go:75 +0x96 ``` This occurs in the context of a service shutdown / host rebalance scenario. This is leading to rollback of service since our organisation has rollback triggers on panics and other errors. ### Reproduction Steps This error is not 100% deterministic it happens due to a race condition between: 1. Shutdown of the topo server (etcd client set to nil) 2. Goroutines still using the topo server (e.g. event watchers) Steps: 1. Run vtgate on some N instances. 2. Shutdown vtgate on a few instances. 3. Check logs of the vtgate host and you'll find these errors. ## The Root Cause The issue is more subtle than just checking if the topo server is nil. The error is occurring in: ``` vitess.io/vitess/go/vt/topo/etcd2topo.(*Server).Get(0xc0005322c0, {0x232a0f8, 0xc004ba8a50}, {0xc00530c7b0?, 0x22?}) vitess.io/vitess/go/vt/topo/etcd2topo/file.go:80 +0x8c ``` Looking at line 80 in `file.go`, we see: ```go resp, err := s.cli.Get(ctx, nodePath) ``` The issue is that `s.cli` (the etcd client) is nil, not the topo server itself. This happens because: 1. During VTGate shutdown, the `Close()` method of `etcd2topo.Server` is called, which does: ``` s.cli.Close() s.cli = nil // Explicitly sets the client to nil ``` 2. However, the topo server object itself is not nil, so our check `if topoServer == nil` passes. 3. When the goroutine tries to use the topo server to call `GetShard()`, internally it tries to access the etcd client which is now nil. ## The Race Condition This is a classic race condition during service restart: 1. Old VTGate instance begins shutting down 2. The etcd client connections are closed and set to nil 3. Goroutines launched by `KeyspaceEventWatcher` are still running 4. These goroutines try to use the topo server whose etcd client is now nil ### Fix We have a fix in our organisation's vitess fork which is doing nil checks in etcd2topo file before accessing it to invoke functions. I'd be happy to upstream it if we conclude that this is a valid problem. If you guys have any other solutions to fix the problem, I'd be happy to implement the same as well. ### Binary Version ```sh vtgate version Version: 21.0.3-SNAPSHOT (Git revision 933f080eb3c6e8c551cf9591029c1e2fb94317b0 branch 'sharded/revert-schema-shard-by-shard') built on Fri Jun 6 13:35:54 IST 2025 by siddharth.singh@siddharth-singh-DM6YX2DV71 using go1.24.1 darwin/arm64 ``` ### Operating System and Environment details ```sh Kernel: Darwin 24.5.0 Architecture: ARM ``` ### Log Fragments ```sh ```
vitessio/vitess#18350.title: Panic during VTGATE shutdown

vitessio/vitess#18426.body:
### Overview

If a filter is passed to a VStream and a filter only matches one of the images, currently only the image which passes the filter is sent, causing downstream consumers to treat the event incorrectly as an insert or delete, when it is actually an update.
vitessio/vitess#18426.title: VStream: if a filter matches only one of before or after images, only the matching image is sent

vitessio/vitess#18427.body:
In a `vitess` (vreplication based` migration, and in the cut-over phase, we take some locks, setup buffering, bring the tables (original & shadow) in sync, stop vreplication, then finally attempt to swap the tables via `RENAME` statement.

The cut-over may fail, and this can happen, and the migration should then resume running and try to cut-over later.

When the cut-over fails, we undo the buffering, we release locks, remove artifacts, etc. However, if the `RENAME` itself fails, vreplication remains `Stopped`. This means any new entries written to the original table are not propagated to the shadow table, essentially making the migration lag. It is then unlikely to ever attempt to cut-over again (unless there's just no more traffic), and will end up failing after 3 hours due to lack of vreplication liveness.


Solution: restart vreplication upon cut-over failure.
vitessio/vitess#18427.title: Online DDL: vreplication does not resume operation after `RENAME` failure

vitessio/vitess#18459.body:
### Summary

This document outlines the implementation of the new proposed `--querylog-emit-on-any-condition-met` to trigger the querylog logging when any of the querylog filter/threshold conditions are met.  This provides a more natural interpretation when multiple querylog flags are specified.

### Background

Currently there are several querylog filter/threshold flags you can specify (e.g. `query log-row-threshold`, `querylog-filter-tag`).  Each flag's purpose is clearly defined when it is used standalone.  However when you specify a combination of those flags, the result is less clear and hard to reason or remember.

For example when both `--querylog-row-threshold` and `--querylog-flilter-tag` are specified, query log-filter-tag processing takes precedence over querylog-row-threshold as seen in the code here: https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/main/go/streamlog/streamlog.go#L278

The same thing also happens when `--querylog-time-threshold` and `--querylog-filter-tag` are both specified.

In many situations, the users just want to have an OR relationship between those flags, they want to see vtgate/vttablet emit querylog either when the row count exceeded or when the query contains the specified filter tag.

### Use Case(s)

- Emit QueryLog when either row-count-threshold or time-threshold is breached
- Emit QueryLog when either time-threshold is breached or query contains a speciic tag
- Emit QueryLog when query has an error (querylog-mode is breached) or time-threshold is breached

### Implementation

#### A new querylog flag

A new querylog flag `--querylog-emit-on-any-condition-met` will be added to emit querylog when any of the querylog threshold/filter/mode is met. 

This would be a boolean flag with default value false to not to change the existing code behavior.

#### Modification of function ShouldEmitLog in streamlog package

Function ShouldEmitLog (https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/main/go/streamlog/streamlog.go#L274) will be enhanced not to short circuit return false when `--querylog-emit-on-any-condition-met` is set to true:

```
func (qlConfig QueryLogConfig) ShouldEmitLog(sql string, rowsAffected, rowsReturned uint64, totalTime time.Duration, hasError bool, emitOnAny bool) bool {
        if emitOnAny {
            return qlConfig.shouldSampleQuery() || 
                        qlConfig.RowThreshold <= max(rowsAffected, rowsReturned) ||
                        qlConfig.TimeThreshold > 0 && qlConfig.TimeThreshold <= totalTime ||
                        qlConfig.FilterTag != "" && strings.Contains(sql, qlConfig.FilterTag) ||
                        qlConfig.Mode == QueryLogModeError  && hasError
        }

        ... existing_code ...
}
```

vitessio/vitess#18488.body: ### Overview of the Issue This started with the issue reported in https://github.com/prisma/prisma/issues/27734, but I've been able to isolate it to a much smaller way to reproduce the problem. The issue is that when a `PREPARE` is executed, in certain cases we end up with no fields returned in answer. I've been debugging this and have written up what I have so far, but I don't think this is something that is just v22 and later specific, but also something that can happen on older versions as well if it triggers using a simple projection in the plan. ### Reproduction Steps ```go package main import ( "database/sql" "fmt" "log" "os" _ "github.com/go-sql-driver/mysql" ) func main() { // Get database URL from environment databaseURL := os.Getenv("DATABASE_URL") if databaseURL == "" { log.Fatal("DATABASE_URL environment variable is required") } // Connect to database db, err := sql.Open("mysql", databaseURL) if err != nil { log.Fatal("Failed to connect to database:", err) } defer db.Close() // Test the connection if err := db.Ping(); err != nil { log.Fatal("Failed to ping database:", err) } query := ` SELECT DISTINCT BINARY table_info.table_name AS table_name FROM information_schema.tables AS table_info JOIN information_schema.columns AS column_info ON BINARY column_info.table_name = BINARY table_info.table_name WHERE table_info.table_schema = ? AND column_info.table_schema = ? ORDER BY BINARY table_info.table_name ` rows, err := db.Query(query, "commerce", "commerce") if err != nil { log.Fatal("Failed to execute query:", err) } defer rows.Close() // Process results fmt.Println("Tables in commerce schema:") fmt.Println("Table Name | Create Options | Comment") fmt.Println("-----------|----------------|--------") for rows.Next() { var tableName, createOptions, tableComment string err := rows.Scan(&tableName, &createOptions, &tableComment) if err != nil { log.Fatal("Failed to scan row:", err) } fmt.Printf("%s | %s | %s\n", tableName, createOptions, tableComment) } if err := rows.Err(); err != nil { log.Fatal("Error iterating rows:", err) } fmt.Println("Query completed successfully!") } ``` When running the above script and dumping the MySQL TCP packets, in Wireshark it's visible what the difference is. #### `main` #### `v21` ### Binary Version ```sh v22 and later ``` ### Operating System and Environment details ```sh - ``` ### Log Fragments ```sh ```

vitessio/vitess#18528.body:
### Overview of the Issue

#### Problem

On at least v19 and probably versions ahead of that, `EmergencyReparentShard` _(which relies on calling the `StopReplicationAndGetStatus` tabletmanager RPC to all tablets)_ fails when any tablet in a shard has MySQL down:
```bash
rpc error: code = Unknown desc = TabletManager.StopReplicationAndGetStatus on REDACTED-0171543854: before status failed: net.Dial(/mnt/vitess/mysql/datadir/mysql.sock) to local server failed: dial unix /mnt/vitess/mysql/datadir/mysql.sock: connect: connection refused (errno 2002) (sqlstate HY000)
```

The `net.Dial` error is `vttablet` attempting to connect to a downed MySQL server on the tablet. In our experience this scenario can happen when:
1. MySQL/InnoDB has crashed/coredumped and cannot come back up
    - In our production we don't let MySQL restart intentionally if it crashes
    - There are also cases where MySQL can crash and cannot start back up
2. MySQL is stopped manually for whatever reason

The ERS code today attempts to, for EVERY tablet _(no matter what)_:
1. Stop Replication and get GTID positions
2. Wait for relay logs to apply
3. Pick a most advanced candidate

This approach is being very careful to understand who has the most relaylog changes, so data loss and errants are not created. However, the failure in the `StopReplicationAndGetStatus` RPC for any tablet halts the ERS at Step number 1 above, which is quite dangerous for availability

#### Solution

Before I propose a solution here, I'll start with some known limitations: what I'm about to propose won't work for tablets with remote MySQL. It's much harder to be certain a network dial error means MySQL is "down"

Now, back to the error we receive: `net.Dial(/mnt/vitess/mysql/datadir/mysql.sock) to local server failed: dial unix /mnt/vitess/mysql/datadir/mysql.sock: connect: connection refused (errno 2002) (sqlstate HY000)`. On a tablet with local MySQL this is a "pretty-good" signal that MySQL is down, but we could be even more certain by checking the PID _(we know this via the pidfile)_ and perhaps other details

So let's imagine we have a strong signal MySQL is down on a tablet that still responds to tabletmanager RPCs. With the caveat that we can't always be 100% certain, if MySQL is down on the tablet, I feel we can infer _(perhaps optionally)_:
1. The MySQL on this tablet is no longer a semi-sync ack'er, because it's down
2. The MySQL on this tablet cannot be the most advanced, because it's down _(can't query it's positions)_
    - There are really odd edge cases where this might not be true, but I'd argue it should cover most regular Vitess users

So TL;DR: let's give `EmergencyReparentShard` logic the context of what tablets have MySQL down or up, so we don't try to wait for them fruitlessly, failing the reparent. We'll assume a down `mysqld` means the tablet cannot be most-advanced

This could be implemented in a few ways:
1. An in-`vttablet` "MySQL Monitor" + modify the RPC response for `StopReplicationAndGetStatus` to include this state
    - Today we get a `nil` response because the RPC errored, so we'd need to adjust that to still return a response, and perhaps include the error in the response
2. The ERS code has a function that reliably matches `mysqld`-down errors, eg: `IsMySQLDown(err error) bool`
    - This could become a game of whack-a-mole, because we're matching on error strings (I believe)
    - This logic would need to match on dial errors _(`mysqld` is down)_ and potentially ignore "timeout" errors, because this doesn't mean `mysqld` is really down

This definitely relies on VTOrc existing in a cluster, to fix tablets with the wrong primary if anything fails here 

Your thoughts are appreciated!

### Reproduction Steps

1. Setup a shard with many tablets
2. `kill -9 $(pidof mysqld)` on one tablet, but ensure the host/pod + `vttablet` remains alive
3. Run an `EmergencyReparentShard` on the given shard
4. Notice the ERS fails on the mysql-down error for a single tablet

### Binary Version

```sh
v19, probably versions above v19
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
Linux
```

### Log Fragments

```sh
rpc error: code = Unknown desc = TabletManager.StopReplicationAndGetStatus on REDACTED-0171543854: before status failed: net.Dial(/mnt/vitess/mysql/datadir/mysql.sock) to local server failed: dial unix /mnt/vitess/mysql/datadir/mysql.sock: connect: connection refused (errno 2002) (sqlstate HY000)
```
vitessio/vitess#18528.title: Bug Report/RFC: `EmergencyReparentShard` fails when `mysqld` is down on any tablet in a shard

vitessio/vitess#18529.body:
### Overview of the Issue

Somewhat related to https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/issues/18528, this issue is to discuss the problem of a single lagging tablet causing ERS to either take an unnecessary amount of time, or to timeout if the lag does not recover in X period of time

The ERS code today attempts to, for EVERY tablet:
1. Stop Replication and get GTID positions
2. Wait for relay logs to apply
3. Pick a most advanced candidate

Let's imagine we have a 4 x tablet shard:
1. `PRIMARY` ✅ 
2. `REPLICA` with negligible lag ✅ 
3. `REPLICA` with negligible lag ✅ 
4. `REPLICA` with 180 seconds of IO-thread lag
    - Example scenario: tablet that just finished restore, tablet that "can't keep up", etc

Today using the example shard above, baring unrelated failures, the ERS code will:
1. Succeed to execute the `StopReplicationAndGetStatus` RPC on all tablets
2. The "wait for relay logs" phase will wait for all tablets. Tablet number 4 _(with 180 seconds of lag)_ will take a long time to execute all relay logs
3. The ERS will potentially/likely fail after exceeding `--wait-replicas-timeout` _(default 15s)_, due to the single lagging replica being so far behind

#### Solution

The solution I'd like to propose is we don't wait for outlier candidates, in terms of replication lag. Using the known GTID positions from `StopReplicationAndGetStatus`, we should be able to be more clever while ensuring the most-advanced candidate(s) are waited for

Proposed solution:
1. Before we reach the "wait for relay logs" phase of the ERS, determine what replicas are the most advanced using the `After` relaylog positions from `StopReplicationAndGetStatus`
    - By the time this RPC returns, replication is stopped everywhere - no moving target
3. Filter-out a minority of least-advanced candidates
    - The least-advanced minority will still apply their relaylogs async, but we won't "wait" for it
5. Only wait for a majority of most-advanced candidates in the "wait for relay logs" phase
    - Today ERS will wait for "all tablets" no matter what
6. Pick a new Primary _(unchanged)_
7. Reparent all tablets _(unchanged)_
    - The least-advanced candidates should catch-up async post-ERS

Your thoughts are appreciated, especially blind-spots in this approach!

### Reproduction Steps

1. Create a shard with many possible candidates for reparent
2. Introduce long-lived IO-thread lag on a replica that is much-larger than `--wait-replicas-timeout` _(default `15s`)_
3. Run an `EmergencyReparentShard` on the test shard
4. Notice the ERS times out, or is significantly more likely to timeout

### Binary Version

```sh
v19+
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
Linux
```

### Log Fragments

```sh

```
vitessio/vitess#18529.title: Bug Report/RFC: lagging tablet(s) can cause `EmergencyReparentShard` to fail

vitessio/vitess#18533.body:
### Overview of the Issue

1. We recently found an issue where there was a duplicate key error in an Online DDL workflow. The error was "valid" in that the migration was incorrectly adding a unique index when their data did not reflect this uniqueness. However the error shows was an "UNKNOWN" empty error from vcopier. 

When we switched to single threaded copy the error surfaced. 

2. There are times when we get errors that don't fit it in the _vt tables. So we truncate them and often lose useful information. Need to ensure we are at least logging it and see if we can perform a more intelligent summarization of the error.

vitessio/vitess#18545.body:
### Overview of the Issue

When running Vitess via vitess-operator, we set the TZ environment variable for all components (vtgate, vttablet).
Inside the containers, the timezone is correctly set. However, when connecting directly to MySQL, the timezone is always UTC.

The reason is that when starting MySQL through the [mysqlctl](https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/main/go/vt/mysqlctl/mysqld.go#L393) wrapper, environment variables are not propagated to the child process.
This is confirmed by:
```
cat /proc/<MYSQL_PID>/environ
```

— the TZ variable is missing.

*Expected behavior:*
mysqlctl should pass the parent process environment variables to the MySQL process.

*Workaround:*
We patched the [buildPath](https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/commit/30333b0e7242e48c384054d518c427cf55f91f40) function to pass all environment variables explicitly. 

### Reproduction Steps

1. [Deploy vitess cluster](https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/commit/376deea36256c7bb611ac8851e7bab425e0e4553), set TZ for vtgate and vttablets
```yaml
gateway:
  extraEnv:
    - name: TZ
       value: Europe/Berlin
tabletPools:
          - cell: zone1
            type: replica
            replicas: 2
            extraEnv:
              - name: TZ
                value: Europe/Berlin
```
2. Exec in mysqld container
```bash
vitess@example-vttablet-zone1-2469782763-bfadd780:/$ ps aux

USER         PID %CPU %MEM    VSZ   RSS TTY      STAT START   TIME COMMAND
vitess         1  0.4  0.1 1793148 45604 ?       Ssl  11:08   0:00 /vt/bin/mysqlctld --db_charset=utf8mb4 --db_dba_user=vt_dba --init_db_sql_file=/vt/secrets/db-init-script/init_db.s
vitess      1196  0.0  0.0 530096  4716 ?        S    11:08   0:00 /bin/sh /usr/bin/mysqld_safe --defaults-file=/vt/vtdataroot/vt_2469782763/my.cnf --basedir=/usr
vitess      1698  2.6  1.9 3214052 460676 ?      Sl   11:08   0:01 /usr/sbin/mysqld --defaults-file=/vt/vtdataroot/vt_2469782763/my.cnf --basedir=/usr --datadir=/vt/vtdataroot/vt_246
vitess      1750  0.3  0.0 529996  4536 pts/0    Ss   11:10   0:00 sh -c (bash || ash || sh)
vitess      1756  0.0  0.0 530012  3616 pts/0    S    11:10   0:00 sh -c (bash || ash || sh)
vitess      1757  1.3  0.0 532024  6868 pts/0    S    11:10   0:00 bash
vitess      1758 66.6  0.0 535788  7828 pts/0    R+   11:10   0:00 ps aux
```

vitessio/vitess#18549.body:
### Feature Description

The mysql protocol has support for arbitrary connection attributes that can be sent from clients to servers.

While the existing implementation supports parsing these attributes on the server side, they are ignored and silently discarded.

A minor enhancement would stash these attributes in the Connection object so anyone using the server could access them. At the same time we might as well add support to the client side to send them, if for nothing else than to facilitate testing.

### Use Case(s)

At Slack we are using the mysql server as part of a client-side forward proxy (see #16045). 

While the proxy itself may not end up landing in the upstream repository, as part of that project we need to be able to send connection-specific attributes to indicate additional information to the proxy.
vitessio/vitess#18549.title: Feature Request: Mysql Protocol Connection Attributres

vitessio/vitess#18554.body:
### Feature Description

Similar to `--queryserver-config-query-timeout` and `QUERY_TIMEOUT_MS`, it would be nice to have a similar query hint for `--queryserver-config-transaction-timeout` like `TRANSACTION_TIMEOUT_MS`.

### Use Case(s)

There has been an effort to reduce this timeout for our clusters, but doing it at a tablet/cluster granularity isn't ideal. Doing at the query level allows app teams to opt-in to the behavior on their own terms. For example, lowering the transaction timeout on web requests while reducing it more slowly for background jobs.
vitessio/vitess#18554.title: Feature Request: Support `TRANSACTION_TIMEOUT_MS` query hint

vitessio/vitess#18562.body:
### Feature Description

When vreplication is throttled for too long, we should emit an error to vstream subscribers instead of waiting for a timeout to occur.

This gives a clear indication of potential issues with data freshness in pipelines. 

I suspect we'd want to enable configuration to define what "too long" means, and figure out a sane default. 

### Use Case(s)

Be explicit of vreplication related issues, rather than failing silently.
vitessio/vitess#18562.title: Feature Request: Emit error for vstream subscribers when Vreplication is  throttled for too long

vitessio/vitess#18564.body:
`UnreachablePrimary` is a scenario where vtorc cannot reach the primary, yet replicas still claim to be replicating.

There can be several causes to this scenario. One known scenario is that the primary is unable t oaccept new connections, but still able to serve existing connections. The replicas, having established some of the oldest connections, will have no trouble fetching data, while new app connections are refused.

I suggest re-introducing an `orchestrator` behavior: do a `stop replication io_thread; start replication io_thread` _once_ on each of the primary's replicas. This closes the TCP connection and attempts to reopen a new one. If the primary does indeed have a new connection allocation issue, this will immediately cause replication to fail on all replicas, and `vtorc` would then proceed to recover `DeadPrimary`. If, on the other hand, the replicas are able to re-establish replication, then `vtorc` does nothing.
vitessio/vitess#18564.title: vtorc: re-introduce emergent restart on replica on `UnreachablePrimary`

vitessio/vitess#18578.body:
### Overview of the Issue

There's quite a few queries where we join `vdiff` and `vdiff_tables` using a condition like `vdiff.id = vdiff_tablet.vdiff_id`.

Unfortunately, the data types between these two columns does not match (taken from `release-21.0`, but this is similar on `main`):

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vdiff
(
    `id`                 bigint(20)   NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
    `vdiff_uuid`         varchar(64)  NOT NULL,
    `workflow`           varbinary(1024)       DEFAULT NULL,
    `keyspace`           varbinary(256)        DEFAULT NULL,
    `shard`              varchar(255) NOT NULL,
    `db_name`            varbinary(1024)       DEFAULT NULL,
    `state`              varbinary(64)         DEFAULT NULL,
    `options`            json                  DEFAULT NULL,
    `created_at`         timestamp    NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    `started_at`         timestamp    NULL     DEFAULT NULL,
    `liveness_timestamp` timestamp    NULL     DEFAULT NULL,
    `completed_at`       timestamp    NULL     DEFAULT NULL,
    `last_error`         varbinary(1024)      DEFAULT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (`id`),
    UNIQUE KEY `uuid_idx` (`vdiff_uuid`),
    KEY `state` (`state`),
    KEY `ks_wf_idx` (`keyspace`(64), `workflow`(64))
) ENGINE = InnoDB CHARSET = utf8mb4
```

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vdiff_table
(
    `vdiff_id`      varchar(64)    NOT NULL,
    `table_name`    varbinary(128) NOT NULL,
    `state`         varbinary(64)           DEFAULT NULL,
    `lastpk`        varbinary(2000)         DEFAULT NULL,
    `table_rows`    bigint(20)     NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',
    `rows_compared` bigint(20)     NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',
    `mismatch`      tinyint(1)     NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',
    `report`        json                    DEFAULT NULL,
    `created_at`    timestamp      NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    `updated_at`    timestamp      NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    PRIMARY KEY (`vdiff_id`, `table_name`)
) ENGINE = InnoDB CHARSET = utf8mb4
```

This means we end up joining a `bigint` column against a `varchar` column, which prevents MySQL from using an index to do the join, and we end up having to scan the full `vdiff_table` contents to perform this join.

This causes performance issues, and some of these queries can even fail with errors like `Out of sort memory, consider increasing server sort buffer size (errno 1038)` if there's additional `ORDER BY` clauses on the queries.

### Reproduction Steps

n/a

### Binary Version

```sh
n/a
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
n/a
```

### Log Fragments

```sh
n/a
```
vitessio/vitess#18578.title: Bug Report: Inefficient join between sidecar tables (`vdiff` / `vdiff_tables`)

vitessio/vitess#18645.body:
### Feature Description

As a VTOrc user, I would like to understand the reason why a recovery was skipped, not just the keyspace/shard that was skipped _(and it's count)_

### Use Case(s)

A VTOrc user that wants to understand why a recovery was skipped

vitessio/vitess#18691.body:
### Feature Description

This issue discusses the removal of the snapshot topologies feature of VTOrc

This feature, if enabled, causes snapshots of instances (on a schedule) to be written to a new sqlite table. This table is never directly read, however. It can be dumped via the diagnostics/debug `/api/database-state` endpoint, but only because this dumps every row of every table

Currently this is enabled by setting the VTOrc flag `--snapshot-topology-interval` to > `0`

Seeing this data isn't used I suggest we drop this code in ~~v24~~ v25 to make the VTOrc codebase smaller. The in-memory nature of this database doesn't make it a great place for historical data anyways

#### Tasks
1. Add deprecation notice to ~~v23~~ v24
    - PR: https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/pull/19070 
3. Deprecate feature in ~~v24~~ v25
    - PR: TBD
 
### Use Case(s)

No particular use case
vitessio/vitess#18691.title: Feature Request: remove `vtorc`snapshot topologies feature

vitessio/vitess#18696.body:
### Overview of the Issue

We have debezium connects to vitess for vstream subscription.  The debezium connector was down for several hours and some tablets were replaced from db backup files during that time.  When debezium connector resumes, the request was routed through vtgate to a new tablet which doesn't have the requested gtid, we would like vtgate to retry to an older tablet which would have the requested gtid, but currently is it always routed to the same new tablet which is in the same cell.

The error message is: 

```
 vstream for byuser/26-27 had three consecutive failures: Code: UNKNOWN
vttablet: rpc error: code = Unknown desc = stream (at source tablet) error @ 013c5ddc-dd89-11ed-b3a1-125a006436b9:1-305627274,fe50e15a-0213-11ee-bfbe-0a048e8090b5:1-340389717: Cannot replicate because the source purged required binary logs. Replicate the missing transactions from elsewhere, or provision a new replica from backup. Consider increasing the source's binary log expiration period. The GTID sets and the missing purged transactions are too long to print in this message. For more information, please see the source's error log or the manual for GTID_SUBTRACT (errno 1236) (sqlstate HY000)
```

### Reproduction Steps

This error handling was actually already implemented in this [PR](https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/pull/14224/files?diff=split&w=0) where we handles the error message related to `GTIDSet Mismatch` where put the tablet into the ignoredTablets so the tablet_picker would try a different tablet the next time.  However the error message we got is a bit different this time: `Cannot replicate because the source purged required binary logs`.  The fix is actually quite similar, we can add the following code around this [line](https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/main/go/vt/vtgate/vstream_manager.go#L878):

```
	if errCode == vtrpcpb.Code_UNKOWN && strings.Contains(err.Error(), "the source purged required binary logs") {
		return true, true
	}
```

### Binary Version

```sh
We are on version 19, but the problem existed on main branch as well.
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
Ubuntu
```

### Log Fragments

```sh

```
vitessio/vitess#18696.title: Bug Report: Handle 'the source purged required binary logs' during vstream subscription

vitessio/vitess#18708.body:
The builtin backup engine has a retry mechanism. If a given file failed to be backed up, for any reason, we retry it at most once.

In some cases, if the failure happened while closing a given file, the retry mechanism will re-upload the file, but the file will never be restorable in the future.

Here is an example when restoring a given file:
```
can't restore from backup: failed to restore file 226 to fts_0000000000000460_000000000000013e_index_4.ibd: failed to copy file contents: open <path>: storage: object doesn't exist
```

That same file had failed once in the previous successful backup:
```
builtinbackupengine.go:873] Backing up file: platform/fts_0000000000000460_000000000000013e_index_4.ibd (attempt 1/2)
builtinbackupengine.go:818] Completed backing up "platform/fts_0000000000000460_000000000000013e_index_4.ibd" (attempt 1/2)
logutil.go:31] log: INFO file closed component=fs name=226 duration=30.108909814s partitions=1 bytes=114688 error="googleapi: got HTTP response code 503 with body: Service Unavailable"
failed to close file 226,platform/fts_0000000000000460_000000000000013e_index_4.ibd
builtinbackupengine.go:873] Backing up file: platform/fts_0000000000000460_000000000000013e_index_4.ibd (attempt 2/2)  
builtinbackupengine.go:818] Completed backing up "platform/fts_0000000000000460_000000000000013e_index_4.ibd" (attempt 2/2)
logutil.go:31] log: INFO file closed component=fs name=226 duration=53.862171ms partitions=1 bytes=114688 error=<nil>
```

This makes me think that when retrying a failed file, we incorrectly overwrite the data. One solution could be to ensure we remove the given file from the remote storage before retrying it, so we always start from a clean slate.
vitessio/vitess#18708.title: Bug Report: builtin backup may create invalid file upon retry

vitessio/vitess#18712.body:
### Overview of the Issue

When the keyspace durability policy is changed from `none` to any other setting that requires semi-sync to be enabled, `vtorc` will notice that change and attempt to "fix" the semi sync settings on the primary by executing a `UndoDemotePrimary` call agains the primary vttablet.

This call will enable semi-sync on the primary, but potentially do so before any of the replicas have semi sync enabled, causing the primary to lock up waiting for acknowledgements. Even when semi sync gets enabled on the replicas, they can't ack any of the changes that are still pending on the primary, so the whole shard locks up.

I believe `vtorc` notices that the primary is locked up, and might attempt to run an ERS, but ERS does not handle semi-sync lockups well right now, and it seems that we can't get out of this broken state.

### Reproduction Steps

N/A

### Binary Version

```sh
N/A
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
N/A
```

### Log Fragments

```sh
N/A
```
vitessio/vitess#18712.title: Bug Report: `vtorc` can cause primary lock up when keyspace durability policy is changed

vitessio/vitess#18743.body:
### Overview of the Issue

When using column based filters in a [VReplication workflow or VTGate VStream](https://vitess.io/docs/reference/vreplication/), and you perform DDL against the source table to modify an existing integer based column to another type (e.g. `int` to `bigint`), the stream can break and require it be redone.

### Reproduction Steps

```
git checkout main && make build

alias vtctldclient='command vtctldclient --server=localhost:15999'

cd examples/local

./101_initial_cluster.sh

vtctldclient Materialize --workflow customer_copy --target-keyspace commerce create --source-keyspace commerce --table-settings '[{"target_table": "customer_copy", "create_ddl": "create table customer_copy (customer_id bigint not null, email varchar(120))", "source_expression": "select email, customer_id from customer"}]'

sleep 5

vtctldclient Materialize --workflow customer_copy --target-keyspace commerce stop

mysql commerce -e 'insert into customer values (1, "mlord@planetscale.com")'
mysql commerce -e "alter table customer modify customer_id int not null auto_increment"
mysql commerce -e 'insert into customer values (2, "mlord@planetscale.com")'

vtctldclient Materialize --workflow customer_copy --target-keyspace commerce start

sleep 5

vtctldclient --compact Materialize --workflow customer_copy --target-keyspace commerce show --include-logs=false | grep message

mysql commerce -e 'select * from customer_copy'
```

You'll see that it fails with this, even though the `email` column is still present in the `customer` table:
```
❯ vtctldclient --compact Materialize --workflow customer_copy --target-keyspace commerce show --include-logs=false | grep message
              "message": "vttablet: rpc error: code = Unknown desc = stream (at source tablet) error @ c1bc72c2-a915-11f0-b289-fd125ea41a96:1-60: Code: FAILED_PRECONDITION\ncolumn email not found in table customer\n\nfailed to build table replication plan for table customer",
```

### Binary Version

```sh
vtgate version Version: 24.0.0-SNAPSHOT (Git revision 3d7f63e1b22df7debad1180cb863d9f06593771b branch 'main') built on Tue Oct 14 15:43:56 UTC 2025 by matt@pslord.local using go1.25.2 darwin/arm64
```

vitessio/vitess#18763.body:
### Overview of the Issue

`EmergencyReparentShard` performs stopping replication on replicas and putting the primary into non-serving mode via `DemotePrimary` in parallel across all the tablets in a cluster / shard.

This makes sense - when calling `EmergencyReparentShard`, the failover is non-graceful and best-effort, as the state of the primary is potentially unknown and we can't wait for `DemotePrimary` to finish "normally".

Unfortunately, we don't actually end up giving `DemotePrimary` any time to properly finish whatever it's doing. When we get back the responses from the replicas, we immediately cancel the context passed to `DemotePrimary`, which will cancels whatever work hasn't been performed yet - effectively preventing the primary from properly being demoted.

This problematic behaviour seems to be hidden by the `SetReplicationSource` command that happens a bit later, where the old primary is attempted to be attached back to new primary, which forces it to switch into `REPLICA` mode.

### Reproduction Steps

N/A

### Binary Version

```sh
N/A
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
N/A
```

### Log Fragments

```sh
N/A
```

### Feature Description

Allow queries mirrored to the target side of a `MoveTables` workflow to bypass denied tables.

### Use Case(s)

Currently `MoveTables MirrorTraffic` has a usability issue: traffic will be denied when executed against the target because of the denied tables specified in the table controls when `MoveTables` workflow was created.

Ideally we would allow those queries to execute in spite of the presence of denied tables. Keep in mind that mirrored queries are currently limited to `SELECT` queries.

vitessio/vitess#18776.body:
### Feature Description

When an existing tablet restarts, it always comes up as the type specified by `--init_tablet_type`, this can make maintenance and upgrades more challenging or lead to unexpected behavior with `vttable` when services restart who's TabletType have changed external to the service definition.

### Use Case(s)

- Upgrading `vttablet` binaries, being able to restart and have types persist.
- allow all tablets to initially deploy as `SPARE`, with types changed to `REPLICA`/`RDONLY` as needed and have types persist on service restarts without edits & reload of `.service` files.
vitessio/vitess#18776.title: Feature Request: Add ability to restore TabletType on restart based on topology.

vitessio/vitess#18781.body: ### Overview of the Issue vReplication keep endlessly retrying a workflow (even if it's always going to be fatal). This means that the state does not transition to "error" and alerting on "error" state will fail. I was hit by this in an environment, that we didn't notice on time that it had failed and therefor the binary logs were already purged and we had to restart the workflow from the start. Transitioning into "error" would be the preferred way after a number of retries for the same operation, and then proper alerting will catch it. ### Reproduction Steps ``` mysql> SELECT message FROM vreplication_log WHERE vrepl_id = 1 AND message LIKE 'error%' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1\G *************************** 1. row *************************** message: error applying event while processing position 143c6157-7ed8-3664-a913-164b2f5696b2:1-1245705118: You do not have the SUPER privilege and binary logging is enabled (you *might* want to use the less safe log_bin_trust_function_creators variable) (errno 1419) (sqlstate HY000) during query: ... ``` this error was hit almost 10k times ``` mysql> SELECT COUNT(*) FROM vreplication_log WHERE vrepl_id = 1 AND message LIKE 'error%'; +----------+ | COUNT(*) | +----------+ | 9373 | +----------+ 1 row in set (0.02 sec) ``` but the state kept always `Running` in the workflow (last 7 days) ### Binary Version ```sh v22.0.0 (build from Sept 4 2025, `da62b83`) ``` ### Operating System and Environment details ```sh PlanetScale environment ``` ### Log Fragments ```sh ```
